message.content:
there's a couple of warning/error dialogs we show to the user - e.g. low battery, low disk space etc 

Can we remove the dialog popups - they're annoying as hell, or at least at an option in settings for someone to select if they want those popups or not

input.prompt: Find all places in this Swift codebase where warning/error/alert dialogs are shown to the user - things like low battery alerts, low disk space warnings, etc. Look for NSAlert, alert(), showAlert, presentAlert, or similar patterns. Also find the AppSettings/Settings model to understand current settings structure. Be thorough - check AppDelegate, monitors, views, etc.

1→import Foundation
     2→
     3→final class AlertRulesEngine {
     4→    enum Rule: String, CaseIterable {
     5→        case cpuUsageHigh
     6→        case cpuTempHigh
     7→        case memoryPressure
     8→        case packetLoss
     9→        case highJitter
    10→        case lowDisk
    11→        case lowBattery
    12→        case networkIncident
    13→    }
    14→
    15→    private let monitors: MonitorCoordinator
    16→    private let historyStore: HistoryStore
    17→    private let settings: AppSettings
    18→    private var timer: Timer?
    19→    private var lastTriggeredAt: [Rule: Date] = [:]
    20→    private var lastIncidentType: NetworkIncidentType?
    21→    private var lastPresentedAlertAt: Date?
    22→
    23→    var onAlert: ((TimelineSeverity, String, String) -> Void)?
    24→
    25→    init(
    26→        monitors: MonitorCoordinator,
    27→        historyStore: HistoryStore,
    28→        settings: AppSettings = .shared
    29→    ) {
    30→        self.monitors = monitors
    31→        self.historyStore = historyStore
    32→        self.settings = settings
    33→    }
    34→
    35→    func start() {
    36→        timer?.invalidate()
    37→        timer = Timer.scheduledTimer(withTimeInterval: 15, repeats: true) { [weak self] _ in
    38→            self?.evaluateRules()
    39→        }
    40→        evaluateRules()
    41→    }
    42→
    43→    func stop() {
    44→        timer?.invalidate()
    45→        timer = nil
    46→    }
    47→
    48→    func evaluateRules() {
    49→        evaluateIncidentState()
    50→        guard settings.alertsEnabled else { return }
    51→
    52→        if let cpu = monitors.cpu.current {
    53→            if cpu.totalUsage >= settings.cpuUsageAlertThreshold {
    54→                trigger(
    55→                    .cpuUsageHigh,
    56→                    severity: .warning,
    57→                    title: "High CPU Usage",
    58→                    message: String(format: "CPU usage is %.0f%%.", cpu.totalUsage)
    59→                )
    60→            }
    61→
    62→            if let temp = cpu.temperature, temp >= settings.cpuTempAlertThreshold {
    63→                trigger(
    64→                    .cpuTempHigh,
    65→                    severity: .critical,
    66→                    title: "High CPU Temperature",
    67→                    message: String(format: "CPU temperature is %.0f°C.", temp)
    68→                )
    69→            }
    70→        }
    71→
    72→        if let memory = monitors.memory.current, memory.pressure == .critical {
    73→            trigger(
    74→                .memoryPressure,
    75→                severity: .critical,
    76→                title: "Critical Memory Pressure",
    77→                message: "Memory pressure is critical. Close heavy apps."
    78→            )
    79→        }
    80→
    81→        if let wifi = monitors.wifi.current {
    82→            let packetLoss = max(wifi.routerPacketLoss ?? 0, wifi.internetPacketLoss ?? 0)
    83→            if packetLoss >= settings.packetLossAlertThreshold {
    84→                trigger(
    85→                    .packetLoss,
    86→                    severity: .warning,
    87→                    title: "Packet Loss Detected",
    88→                    message: String(format: "Packet loss is %.1f%%.", packetLoss)
    89→                )
    90→            }
    91→
    92→            let jitter = max(wifi.routerJitter ?? 0, wifi.internetJitter ?? 0)
    93→            if jitter >= settings.jitterAlertThreshold {
    94→                trigger(
    95→                    .highJitter,
    96→                    severity: .warning,
    97→                    title: "High Jitter Detected",
    98→                    message: String(format: "Jitter is %.0f ms.", jitter)
    99→                )
   100→            }
   101→        }
   102→
   103→        if let firstVolume = monitors.disk.current?.volumes.first {
   104→            let freePercent = firstVolume.total > 0 ? (Double(firstVolume.free) / Double(firstVolume.total) * 100) : 100
   105→            if freePercent <= settings.lowDiskFreePercentThreshold {
   106→                trigger(
   107→                    .lowDisk,
   108→                    severity: .critical,
   109→                    title: "Low Disk Space",
   110→                    message: String(format: "%@ has %.1f%% free space.", firstVolume.name, freePercent)
   111→                )
   112→            }
   113→        }
   114→
   115→        if settings.lowBatteryAlertsEnabled,
   116→           let battery = monitors.battery.current,
   117→           battery.powerSource == .battery,
   118→           !battery.isCharging,
   119→           battery.chargePercent <= settings.lowBatteryAlertThreshold {
   120→            trigger(
   121→                .lowBattery,
   122→                severity: .critical,
   123→                title: "Low Battery",
   124→                message: String(format: "Battery is at %.0f%%.", battery.chargePercent)
   125→            )
   126→        }
   127→    }
   128→
   129→    private func evaluateIncidentState() {
   130→        let incident = NetworkIncidentAnalyzer.detect(from: monitors.wifi.current)
   131→        let incidentType = incident?.type
   132→
   133→        if incidentType != lastIncidentType {
   134→            if let incident {
   135→                historyStore.recordEvent(TimelineEvent(
   136→                    severity: .warning,
   137→                    category: .network,
   138→                    title: incident.title,
   139→                    message: incident.hint
   140→                ))
   141→
   142→                trigger(
   143→                    .networkIncident,
   144→                    severity: .warning,
   145→                    title: incident.title,
   146→                    message: incident.hint
   147→                )
   148→            } else if lastIncidentType != nil {
   149→                historyStore.recordEvent(TimelineEvent(
   150→                    severity: .info,
   151→                    category: .network,
   152→                    title: "Network Recovered",
   153→                    message: "Connectivity checks are back to normal."
   154→                ))
   155→            }
   156→            lastIncidentType = incidentType
   157→        }
   158→    }
   159→
   160→    private func trigger(
   161→        _ rule: Rule,
   162→        severity: TimelineSeverity,
   163→        title: String,
   164→        message: String
   165→    ) {
   166→        let now = Date()
   167→        let cooldown = settings.alertsCooldownMinutes * 60
   168→        if let last = lastTriggeredAt[rule], now.timeIntervalSince(last) < cooldown {
   169→            return
   170→        }
   171→
   172→        lastTriggeredAt[rule] = now
   173→        historyStore.recordEvent(TimelineEvent(
   174→            severity: severity,
   175→            category: category(for: rule),
   176→            title: title,
   177→            message: message
   178→        ))
   179→
   180→        guard !isInQuietHours() else { return }
   181→
   182→        // Keep a small global spacing between popups from different rules.
   183→        if let lastPresentedAlertAt, now.timeIntervalSince(lastPresentedAlertAt) < 5 {
   184→            return
   185→        }
   186→
   187→        lastPresentedAlertAt = now
   188→        onAlert?(severity, title, message)
   189→    }
   190→
   191→    private func category(for rule: Rule) -> TimelineCategory {
   192→        switch rule {
   193→        case .cpuUsageHigh, .cpuTempHigh, .memoryPressure:
   194→            return .alert
   195→        case .packetLoss, .highJitter, .networkIncident:
   196→            return .network
   197→        case .lowDisk:
   198→            return .disk
   199→        case .lowBattery:
   200→            return .battery
   201→        }
   202→    }
   203→
   204→    private func isInQuietHours() -> Bool {
   205→        guard settings.alertsQuietHoursEnabled else { return false }
   206→
   207→        let calendar = Calendar.current
   208→        let hour = calendar.component(.hour, from: Date())
   209→        let start = Int(settings.alertsQuietHoursStartHour)
   210→        let end = Int(settings.alertsQuietHoursEndHour)
   211→
   212→        if start == end {
   213→            return true
   214→        }
   215→
   216→        if start < end {
   217→            return hour >= start && hour < end
   218→        }
   219→
   220→        // Overnight interval (e.g. 22 -> 7)
   221→        return hour >= start || hour < end
   222→    }
   223→}
   224→

1→import Foundation
     2→
     3→enum TimelineSeverity: String, Codable, CaseIterable {
     4→    case info
     5→    case warning
     6→    case critical
     7→}
     8→
     9→enum TimelineCategory: String, Codable, CaseIterable {
    10→    case alert
    11→    case network
    12→    case disk
    13→    case battery
    14→    case process
    15→    case system
    16→}
    17→
    18→struct TimelineEvent: Identifiable, Codable {
    19→    let id: UUID
    20→    let timestamp: Date
    21→    let severity: TimelineSeverity
    22→    let category: TimelineCategory
    23→    let title: String
    24→    let message: String
    25→
    26→    init(
    27→        id: UUID = UUID(),
    28→        timestamp: Date = Date(),
    29→        severity: TimelineSeverity,
    30→        category: TimelineCategory,
    31→        title: String,
    32→        message: String
    33→    ) {
    34→        self.id = id
    35→        self.timestamp = timestamp
    36→        self.severity = severity
    37→        self.category = category
    38→        self.title = title
    39→        self.message = message
    40→    }
    41→}
    42→
    43→struct MetricSnapshot: Identifiable, Codable {
    44→    let id: UUID
    45→    let timestamp: Date
    46→    let cpuUsage: Double
    47→    let memoryUsedPercent: Double
    48→    let memoryPressureScore: Double
    49→    let networkDownload: Double
    50→    let networkUpload: Double
    51→    let diskFreePercent: Double
    52→    let batteryPercent: Double?
    53→    let batteryIsCharging: Bool?
    54→
    55→    init(
    56→        id: UUID = UUID(),
    57→        timestamp: Date = Date(),
    58→        cpuUsage: Double,
    59→        memoryUsedPercent: Double,
    60→        memoryPressureScore: Double,
    61→        networkDownload: Double,
    62→        networkUpload: Double,
    63→        diskFreePercent: Double,
    64→        batteryPercent: Double?,
    65→        batteryIsCharging: Bool?
    66→    ) {
    67→        self.id = id
    68→        self.timestamp = timestamp
    69→        self.cpuUsage = cpuUsage
    70→        self.memoryUsedPercent = memoryUsedPercent
    71→        self.memoryPressureScore = memoryPressureScore
    72→        self.networkDownload = networkDownload
    73→        self.networkUpload = networkUpload
    74→        self.diskFreePercent = diskFreePercent
    75→        self.batteryPercent = batteryPercent

    80→enum TimelineRange: String, CaseIterable, Identifiable {
    81→    case last24h = "24h"
    82→    case last7d = "7d"

     3→struct TimelineSectionView: View {

     6→
     7→    private var events: [TimelineEvent] {
     8→        historyStore
     9→            .events(in: range)
    10→            .sorted { $0.timestamp > $1.timestamp }
    11→    }
    12→

    68→

   115→    private var latestSnapshotAge: String {

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the alert system. Let me create a final summary document.

## Alert Dialogs & Warnings - Comprehensive Report

Based on my thorough search of the WhatTheLoad codebase, here are all the places where warning/error/alert dialogs are shown to users:

### 1. **Alert Presentation Points**

#### AppDelegate.swift (Lines 230-261)
**Two main alert display methods:**

- **`showLowBatteryAlert(metrics:threshold:)`** (Lines 230-248)
  - NSAlert with `.warning` style
  - Triggered when: Battery level drops below user-configured threshold while on battery power and not charging
  - Shows: Current battery %, threshold %, and estimated time remaining
  - Called every 15 seconds via `checkLowBatteryAlert()` but shown only once until battery recovers

- **`showBlockingAlert(severity:title:message:)`** (Lines 250-261)
  - Generic NSAlert handler for all rule-based alerts
  - Alert style is `.critical` for critical severity, `.warning` otherwise
  - Runs on main thread via `DispatchQueue.main.async`
  - Called by the AlertRulesEngine for all non-low-battery alerts

#### ProcessesSectionView.swift (Lines 82-91)
**SwiftUI-style alert for process termination:**
- `.alert()` modifier with `PendingSignalAction` state
- Two actions: "Quit Process" (SIGTERM) and "Force Quit" (SIGKILL)
- Shows process name, PID, and action type
- Triggered via context menu on process rows

#### DiskSectionView.swift (Lines 24-53)
**Two confirmation dialogs:**

1. **Clear Library Caches Alert** (Lines 24-31)
   - SwiftUI `.alert()` modifier
   - Warns users that ~/Library/Caches will be removed
   - Has Cancel and "Clear" (destructive) buttons
   - State: `showingClearCachesConfirmation`

2. **Move Item to Trash Alert** (Lines 32-53)
   - SwiftUI `.alert()` modifier  
   - Shows item name and path
   - Has Cancel and "Move to Trash" (destructive) buttons
   - State: Binding based on `pendingTrashTarget`

---

### 2. **Alert Rules Engine** (AlertRulesEngine.swift)

The **AlertRulesEngine** is the central system that monitors metrics and triggers alerts. It runs on a 15-second timer and checks these rules:

| Rule | Severity | Triggered When | Message Format |
|------|----------|---|---|
| **cpuUsageHigh** | warning | CPU ≥ threshold (default 90%) | "CPU usage is X%." |
| **cpuTempHigh** | critical | CPU temp ≥ threshold (default 90°C) | "CPU temperature is X°C." |
| **memoryPressure** | critical | Memory pressure = critical | "Memory pressure is critical. Close heavy apps." |
| **packetLoss** | warning | Packet loss ≥ threshold (default 3%) | "Packet loss is X%." |
| **highJitter** | warning | Jitter ≥ threshold (default 30ms) | "Jitter is X ms." |
| **lowDisk** | critical | Free disk space ≤ threshold (default 10%) | "[Volume] has X% free space." |
| **lowBattery** | critical | Battery ≤ threshold (default 20%) | "Battery is at X%." |
| **networkIncident** | warning | Network conditions degrade | "Network incident detected." |

**Key Features:**
- Cooldown system: Prevents same alert from retriggering within configured interval (default 10 minutes)
- Quiet hours: Alerts suppressed during user-defined hours (default 22:00-07:00)
- 5-second minimum spacing between different rule alerts to avoid alert spam
- All triggered alerts logged to HistoryStore even if not displayed due to quiet hours/cooldown

---

### 3. **AppSettings Model** (AppSettings.swift)

The settings structure controlling all alert behavior:

**Alert Enable/Disable:**
```swift
@AppStorage("alertsEnabled") var alertsEnabled: Bool = true
@AppStorage("lowBatteryAlertsEnabled") var lowBatteryAlertsEnabled: Bool = true
```

**Alert Thresholds:**
```swift
@AppStorage("cpuUsageAlertThreshold") var cpuUsageAlertThreshold: Double = 90.0
@AppStorage("cpuTempAlertThreshold") var cpuTempAlertThreshold: Double = 90.0
@AppStorage("packetLossAlertThreshold") var packetLossAlertThreshold: Double = 3.0
@AppStorage("jitterAlertThreshold") var jitterAlertThreshold: Double = 30.0
@AppStorage("lowDiskFreePercentThreshold") var lowDiskFreePercentThreshold: Double = 10.0
@AppStorage("lowBatteryAlertThreshold") var lowBatteryAlertThreshold: Double = 20.0
```

**Alert Behavior:**
```swift
@AppStorage("alertsCooldownMinutes") var alertsCooldownMinutes: Double = 10.0
@AppStorage("alertsQuietHoursEnabled") var alertsQuietHoursEnabled: Bool = false
@AppStorage("alertsQuietHoursStartHour") var alertsQuietHoursStartHour: Double = 22.0
@AppStorage("alertsQuietHoursEndHour") var alertsQuietHoursEndHour: Double = 7.0
```

**Battery Automation:**
```swift
@AppStorage("batteryAutomationEnabled") var batteryAutomationEnabled: Bool = true
@AppStorage("batteryAutomationThreshold") var batteryAutomationThreshold: Double = 20.0
@AppStorage("batteryAutomationAutoOpenSettings") var batteryAutomationAutoOpenSettings: Bool = false
@AppStorage("powerSaveModeActive") var powerSaveModeActive: Bool = false
```

---

### 4. **Alert History & Timeline System**

**HistoryStore.swift**: Maintains persistent record of all alerts
- Stores `TimelineEvent` objects with severity, category, title, message
- 7-day retention window
- Max 5000 events kept in memory
- Persisted to JSON at `~/Library/Application Support/WhatTheLoad/timeline_events.json`

**TimelineModels.swift**: Defines alert data structures
- `TimelineSeverity`: info, warning, critical
- `TimelineCategory`: alert, network, disk, battery, process, system
- `TimelineEvent`: Identified event with timestamp, severity, category, title, message

**TimelineSectionView.swift**: UI for viewing alert history
- Shows last 24 hours or 7 days of events
- Summary cards showing alert counts by type
- Trend graphs from metric snapshots

---

### 5. **Battery-Specific Alert System**

AppDelegate has two separate battery monitoring systems:

1. **Low Battery Alert** (every 15 seconds)
   - Checks if battery is below threshold while on battery power
   - Shows NSAlert popup with "Dismiss" button
   - Prevents duplicate alerts until battery recovers above (threshold + 5%)

2. **Battery Automation** (every 20 seconds)
   - Automatically enables Power Save Mode when battery ≤ threshold
   - Can optionally auto-open System Settings battery page
   - Disables Power Save Mode when battery recovers above (threshold + 5%)
   - Records all mode changes to timeline

---

### File Locations (Absolute Paths)

| Component | Path |
|-----------|------|
| AppDelegate | `/Users/modestnerd/Developer/Projects/WhatTheLoad/WhatTheLoad/App/AppDelegate.swift` |
| AlertRulesEngine | `/Users/modestnerd/Developer/Projects/WhatTheLoad/WhatTheLoad/Services/AlertRulesEngine.swift` |
| AppSettings | `/Users/modestnerd/Developer/Projects/WhatTheLoad/WhatTheLoad/Settings/AppSettings.swift` |
| SettingsView | `/Users/modestnerd/Developer/Projects/WhatTheLoad/WhatTheLoad/Settings/SettingsView.swift` |
| ProcessesSectionView | `/Users/modestnerd/Developer/Projects/WhatTheLoad/WhatTheLoad/Views/Sections/ProcessesSectionView.swift` |
| DiskSectionView | `/Users/modestnerd/Developer/Projects/WhatTheLoad/WhatTheLoad/Views/Sections/DiskSectionView.swift` |
| HistoryStore | `/Users/modestnerd/Developer/Projects/WhatTheLoad/WhatTheLoad/Services/HistoryStore.swift` |
| TimelineModels | `/Users/modestnerd/Developer/Projects/WhatTheLoad/WhatTheLoad/Models/TimelineModels.swift` |
| TimelineSectionView | `/Users/modestnerd/Developer/Projects/WhatTheLoad/WhatTheLoad/Views/Sections/TimelineSectionView.swift` |

---

### Alert Flow Diagram

```
Monitors (CPU, Memory, Disk, WiFi, Battery) 
    ↓
AlertRulesEngine (evaluates rules every 15s)
    ↓
Check: alertsEnabled? → Check cooldown? → Check quiet hours?
    ↓ (all pass)
AppDelegate.showBlockingAlert() → NSAlert popup (main thread)
    ↓
HistoryStore.recordEvent() → Timeline + JSON persistence
    ↓
TimelineSectionView displays history
```

Good news - there's already an `alertsEnabled` toggle. The issue is that even with fine-grained control, the alerts still show as blocking `NSAlert` popups. Let me look at the key files to implement a "notification style" setting.

file.content:
import SwiftUI
import AppKit

class AppDelegate: NSObject, NSApplicationDelegate {
    private var statusItem: NSStatusItem!
    private var popover: NSPopover!
    private let monitors = MonitorCoordinator.shared
    private let settings = AppSettings.shared
    private let historyStore = HistoryStore.shared
    private var menuBarController: MenuBarController!
    private var alertRulesEngine: AlertRulesEngine?
    private var lowBatteryAlertTimer: Timer?
    private var lowBatteryAlertShown = false
    private var batteryAutomationTimer: Timer?
    private var batterySettingsOpenedAt: Date?

    func applicationDidFinishLaunching(_ notification: Notification) {
        NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.accessory)
        monitors.setPowerSaveMode(settings.powerSaveModeActive)

        // Start monitoring
        monitors.startAll()
        historyStore.start(with: monitors)

        // Create status item
        statusItem = NSStatusBar.system.statusItem(withLength: NSStatusItem.variableLength)

        if let button = statusItem.button {
            button.action = #selector(handleStatusItemClick(_:))
            button.target = self
            button.sendAction(on: [.leftMouseUp, .rightMouseUp])
        }

        // Create and start menu bar controller
        menuBarController = MenuBarController(statusItem: statusItem, monitors: monitors)
        menuBarController.startUpdating()

        startAlertRulesEngine()
        startLowBatteryAlertMonitoring()
        startBatteryAutomationMonitoring()

        // Create popover
        popover = NSPopover()
        popover.contentSize = NSSize(width: 360, height: 520)
        popover.behavior = .transient
        popover.contentViewController = NSHostingController(
            rootView: DashboardView(monitors: monitors, historyStore: historyStore)
        )
    }

content.content:
1→import Foundation
     2→
     3→final class AlertRulesEngine {
     4→    enum Rule: String, CaseIterable {
     5→        case cpuUsageHigh
     6→        case cpuTempHigh
     7→        case memoryPressure
     8→        case packetLoss
     9→        case highJitter
    10→        case lowDisk
    11→        case lowBattery
    12→        case networkIncident
    13→    }
    14→
    15→    private let monitors: MonitorCoordinator
    16→    private let historyStore: HistoryStore
    17→    private let settings: AppSettings
    18→    private var timer: Timer?
    19→    private var lastTriggeredAt: [Rule: Date] = [:]
    20→    private var lastIncidentType: NetworkIncidentType?
    21→    private var lastPresentedAlertAt: Date?
    22→
    23→    var onAlert: ((TimelineSeverity, String, String) -> Void)?
    24→
    25→    init(
    26→        monitors: MonitorCoordinator,
    27→        historyStore: HistoryStore,
    28→        settings: AppSettings = .shared
    29→    ) {
    30→        self.monitors = monitors
    31→        self.historyStore = historyStore
    32→        self.settings = settings
    33→    }
    34→
    35→    func start() {
    36→        timer?.invalidate()
    37→        timer = Timer.scheduledTimer(withTimeInterval: 15, repeats: true) { [weak self] _ in
    38→            self?.evaluateRules()
    39→        }
    40→        evaluateRules()
    41→    }
    42→
    43→    func stop() {
    44→        timer?.invalidate()
    45→        timer = nil
    46→    }
    47→
    48→    func evaluateRules() {
    49→        evaluateIncidentState()
    50→        guard settings.alertsEnabled else { return }
    51→
    52→        if let cpu = monitors.cpu.current {
    53→            if cpu.totalUsage >= settings.cpuUsageAlertThreshold {
    54→                trigger(
    55→                    .cpuUsageHigh,
    56→                    severity: .warning,
    57→                    title: "High CPU Usage",
    58→                    message: String(format: "CPU usage is %.0f%%.", cpu.totalUsage)
    59→                )
    60→            }
    61→
    62→            if let temp = cpu.temperature, temp >= settings.cpuTempAlertThreshold {
    63→                trigger(
    64→                    .cpuTempHigh,
    65→                    severity: .critical,
    66→                    title: "High CPU Temperature",
    67→                    message: String(format: "CPU temperature is %.0f°C.", temp)
    68→                )
    69→            }
    70→        }
    71→
    72→        if let memory = monitors.memory.current, memory.pressure == .critical {
    73→            trigger(
    74→                .memoryPressure,
    75→                severity: .critical,
    76→                title: "Critical Memory Pressure",
    77→                message: "Memory pressure is critical. Close heavy apps."
    78→            )
    79→        }
    80→
    81→        if let wifi = monitors.wifi.current {
    82→            let packetLoss = max(wifi.routerPacketLoss ?? 0, wifi.internetPacketLoss ?? 0)
    83→            if packetLoss >= settings.packetLossAlertThreshold {
    84→                trigger(
    85→                    .packetLoss,
    86→                    severity: .warning,
    87→                    title: "Packet Loss Detected",
    88→                    message: String(format: "Packet loss is %.1f%%.", packetLoss)
    89→                )
    90→            }
    91→
    92→            let jitter = max(wifi.routerJitter ?? 0, wifi.internetJitter ?? 0)
    93→            if jitter >= settings.jitterAlertThreshold {
    94→                trigger(
    95→                    .highJitter,
    96→                    severity: .warning,
    97→                    title: "High Jitter Detected",
    98→                    message: String(format: "Jitter is %.0f ms.", jitter)
    99→                )
   100→            }
   101→        }
   102→
   103→        if let firstVolume = monitors.disk.current?.volumes.first {
   104→            let freePercent = firstVolume.total > 0 ? (Double(firstVolume.free) / Double(firstVolume.total) * 100) : 100
   105→            if freePercent <= settings.lowDiskFreePercentThreshold {
   106→                trigger(
   107→                    .lowDisk,
   108→                    severity: .critical,
   109→                    title: "Low Disk Space",
   110→                    message: String(format: "%@ has %.1f%% free space.", firstVolume.name, freePercent)
   111→                )
   112→            }
   113→        }
   114→
   115→        if settings.lowBatteryAlertsEnabled,
   116→           let battery = monitors.battery.current,
   117→           battery.powerSource == .battery,
   118→           !battery.isCharging,
   119→           battery.chargePercent <= settings.lowBatteryAlertThreshold {
   120→            trigger(
   121→                .lowBattery,
   122→                severity: .critical,
   123→                title: "Low Battery",
   124→                message: String(format: "Battery is at %.0f%%.", battery.chargePercent)
   125→            )
   126→        }
   127→    }
   128→
   129→    private func evaluateIncidentState() {
   130→        let incident = NetworkIncidentAnalyzer.detect(from: monitors.wifi.current)
   131→        let incidentType = incident?.type
   132→
   133→        if incidentType != lastIncidentType {
   134→            if let incident {
   135→                historyStore.recordEvent(TimelineEvent(
   136→                    severity: .warning,
   137→                    category: .network,
   138→                    title: incident.title,
   139→                    message: incident.hint
   140→                ))
   141→
   142→                trigger(
   143→                    .networkIncident,
   144→                    severity: .warning,
   145→                    title: incident.title,
   146→                    message: incident.hint
   147→                )
   148→            } else if lastIncidentType != nil {
   149→                historyStore.recordEvent(TimelineEvent(
   150→                    severity: .info,
   151→                    category: .network,
   152→                    title: "Network Recovered",
   153→                    message: "Connectivity checks are back to normal."
   154→                ))
   155→            }
   156→            lastIncidentType = incidentType
   157→        }
   158→    }
   159→
   160→    private func trigger(
   161→        _ rule: Rule,
   162→        severity: TimelineSeverity,
   163→        title: String,
   164→        message: String
   165→    ) {
   166→        let now = Date()
   167→        let cooldown = settings.alertsCooldownMinutes * 60
   168→        if let last = lastTriggeredAt[rule], now.timeIntervalSince(last) < cooldown {
   169→            return
   170→        }
   171→
   172→        lastTriggeredAt[rule] = now
   173→        historyStore.recordEvent(TimelineEvent(
   174→            severity: severity,
   175→            category: category(for: rule),
   176→            title: title,
   177→            message: message
   178→        ))
   179→
   180→        guard !isInQuietHours() else { return }
   181→
   182→        // Keep a small global spacing between popups from different rules.
   183→        if let lastPresentedAlertAt, now.timeIntervalSince(lastPresentedAlertAt) < 5 {
   184→            return
   185→        }
   186→
   187→        lastPresentedAlertAt = now
   188→        onAlert?(severity, title, message)
   189→    }
   190→
   191→    private func category(for rule: Rule) -> TimelineCategory {
   192→        switch rule {
   193→        case .cpuUsageHigh, .cpuTempHigh, .memoryPressure:
   194→            return .alert
   195→        case .packetLoss, .highJitter, .networkIncident:
   196→            return .network
   197→        case .lowDisk:
   198→            return .disk
   199→        case .lowBattery:
   200→            return .battery
   201→        }
   202→    }
   203→
   204→    private func isInQuietHours() -> Bool {
   205→        guard settings.alertsQuietHoursEnabled else { return false }
   206→
   207→        let calendar = Calendar.current
   208→        let hour = calendar.component(.hour, from: Date())
   209→        let start = Int(settings.alertsQuietHoursStartHour)
   210→        let end = Int(settings.alertsQuietHoursEndHour)
   211→
   212→        if start == end {
   213→            return true
   214→        }
   215→
   216→        if start < end {
   217→            return hour >= start && hour < end
   218→        }
   219→
   220→        // Overnight interval (e.g. 22 -> 7)
   221→        return hour >= start || hour < end
   222→    }
   223→}
   224→

That's a **code coverage / profiling data file** generated by LLVM. It gets created when you build with code coverage enabled (e.g. running tests with coverage in Xcode). It contains raw profiling counters that can be processed into coverage reports.

It's a build artifact and shouldn't be checked into source control. Want me to add it to `.gitignore`?

1→import AppKit
     2→
     3→class SparklineRenderer {
     4→    static func render(data: [Double], size: NSSize, color: NSColor = .systemGreen) -> NSImage? {
     5→        guard !data.isEmpty else { return nil }
     6→
     7→        let image = NSImage(size: size)
     8→        image.lockFocus()
     9→
    10→        let context = NSGraphicsContext.current?.cgContext
    11→        context?.clear(CGRect(origin: .zero, size: size))
    12→
    13→        // Find min/max for scaling
    14→        let maxValue = data.max() ?? 100
    15→        let minValue = data.min() ?? 0
    16→        let range = max(maxValue - minValue, 1)
    17→
    18→        // Create path
    19→        let path = NSBezierPath()
    20→        let xStep = size.width / CGFloat(max(data.count - 1, 1))
    21→
    22→        for (index, value) in data.enumerated() {
    23→            let x = CGFloat(index) * xStep
    24→            let normalizedValue = (value - minValue) / range
    25→            let y = CGFloat(normalizedValue) * size.height
    26→
    27→            if index == 0 {
    28→                path.move(to: NSPoint(x: x, y: y))
    29→            } else {
    30→                path.line(to: NSPoint(x: x, y: y))
    31→            }
    32→        }
    33→
    34→        // Draw gradient fill
    35→        let gradientColors = [
    36→            color.withAlphaComponent(0.6),
    37→            color.withAlphaComponent(0.0)
    38→        ]
    39→        let gradient = NSGradient(colors: gradientColors)
    40→
    41→        context?.saveGState()
    42→
    43→        // Create fill path
    44→        let fillPath = path.copy() as! NSBezierPath
    45→        fillPath.line(to: NSPoint(x: size.width, y: 0))
    46→        fillPath.line(to: NSPoint(x: 0, y: 0))
    47→        fillPath.close()
    48→
    49→        fillPath.addClip()
    50→        gradient?.draw(from: NSPoint(x: 0, y: size.height), to: NSPoint(x: 0, y: 0), options: [])
    51→
    52→        context?.restoreGState()
    53→
    54→        // Draw line
    55→        color.setStroke()
    56→        path.lineWidth = 1.5
    57→        path.stroke()
    58→
    59→        image.unlockFocus()
    60→        return image
    61→    }
    62→
    63→    static func colorForValue(_ value: Double) -> NSColor {
    64→        switch value {
    65→        case 0..<50: return .systemGreen
    66→        case 50..<80: return .systemOrange
    67→        default: return .systemRed
    68→        }
    69→    }
    70→}
    71→

    19→        let memory: Double

    27→    var systemUptime: String {
    28→        let uptime = ProcessInfo.processInfo.systemUptime
    29→        let days = Int(uptime / 86400)
    30→        let hours = Int((uptime.truncatingRemainder(dividingBy: 86400)) / 3600)
    31→        let minutes = Int((uptime.truncatingRemainder(dividingBy: 3600)) / 60)
    32→        return "\(days)d \(hours)h \(minutes)m"
    33→    }
    34→
    35→    func startAll() {

    37→    }
    38→

    42→    }
    43→

    46→        isPowerSaveMode = enabled

    62→        cpu.start(interval: profile.cpu)
    63→        memory.start(interval: profile.memory)
    64→        network.start(interval: profile.network)
    65→        wifi.start(interval: profile.wifi)
    66→        disk.start(interval: profile.disk)
    67→        processes.start(interval: profile.processes)
    68→        battery.start(interval: profile.battery)
    69→    }

Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed analysis document:

## Menu Bar Implementation Analysis - WhatTheLoad

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here's the complete breakdown of the menu bar implementation:

### 1. **Currently Displayed Metrics in Menu Bar**

The menu bar displays the following metrics in **text format only**:

- **Download Speed**: Unicode arrow (↓) followed by formatted speed (e.g., "↓24.5M/s")
- **Upload Speed**: Unicode arrow (↑) followed by formatted speed (e.g., "↑1.2M/s")
- **Battery Information** (when available):
  - Battery icon (SF Symbol) - changes based on charge level
  - Time remaining in H:MM format (e.g., "2:45")
  - OR "Full" / "AC" if plugged in

**Key Detail**: The menu bar currently shows **network speeds only**, not CPU or memory usage despite having settings for sparklines.

### 2. **Rendering Method**

The rendering uses **NSAttributedString with monospaced font** built via `updateStatusItem()` in MenuBarController.swift:

```swift
// Font: NSFont.monospacedSystemFont(ofSize: 11, weight: .regular)
// Color: NSColor.white
```

**Construction Process**:
1. Each element is built as a separate `NSAttributedString` with base attributes
2. Elements are appended sequentially: arrow, speed value, space, icon, time text
3. Battery icon is rendered using `NSTextAttachment` (SF Symbols system symbols)
4. Final string assigned to `button.attributedTitle`

**Update Frequency**: Every 1 second via Timer (line 15 in MenuBarController)

### 3. **How Status Item Button Content is Built**

Located in `/Users/modestnerd/Developer/Projects/WhatTheLoad/WhatTheLoad/MenuBar/MenuBarController.swift` (lines 26-61):

```
Flow:
1. Get current network metrics from monitors.network.current
2. Create NSMutableAttributedString with base attributes (white, monospace, 11pt)
3. Build components in order:
   - "↓" + download speed (formatted with compactRate)
   - " " (space)
   - "↑" + upload speed (formatted with compactRate)
   - Optional battery section:
     - "  " (two spaces)
     - Battery SF Symbol (13x13 px with -2 offset)
     - " " (space)
     - Time remaining text (formatted as H:MM)
4. Assign to button.attributedTitle
```

**Key Helper Methods**:
- `compactRate(_ bytesPerSecond: Double)` - Converts bytes/s to KB/s, MB/s, GB/s with smart decimal places
- `batteryTimeText(for:)` - Returns remaining time, "Full", or "AC"
- `batteryImage(for:)` - Returns appropriate SF Symbol based on charge level (battery.25, battery.50, battery.75, battery.100, or battery.100.bolt)
- `formatShortTime(_ seconds:)` - Formats seconds to H:MM format

### 4. **AppSettings Menu Bar Configuration**

Located in `/Users/modestnerd/Developer/Projects/WhatTheLoad/WhatTheLoad/Settings/AppSettings.swift` (lines 16-18):

```swift
@AppStorage("showMenuBarSparkline") var showMenuBarSparkline: Bool = true
@AppStorage("showMenuBarNetworkText") var showMenuBarNetworkText: Bool = true
@AppStorage("menuBarSparklineMetric") var menuBarSparklineMetric: String = "cpu"
```

**Status**: These settings exist in AppSettings and are exposed in SettingsView UI (lines 78-96) with:
- Toggle: "Show Sparkline" 
- Toggle: "Show Network Speed"
- Picker: "Sparkline Metric" (CPU / Memory / Network)

**However**: These settings are **currently NOT USED** in MenuBarController - the menu bar always displays network speeds and never displays sparklines.

### 5. **SparklineRenderer Available But Unused**

The codebase includes a complete `SparklineRenderer` class at `/Users/modestnerd/Developer/Projects/WhatTheLoad/WhatTheLoad/MenuBar/SparklineRenderer.swift`:

```swift
static func render(data: [Double], size: NSSize, color: NSColor) -> NSImage?
```

**Features**:
- Renders sparklines to NSImage with gradient fill
- Supports color selection based on value thresholds (green <50%, orange 50-80%, red >80%)
- Handles data scaling and normalization
- Returns template images suitable for menu bar use

**Current Use**: Only used in dashboard views (e.g., CPUSectionView), not in menu bar.

### 6. **Available Data Sources**

All monitors store history (up to 120 samples):
- `CPUMonitor`: `current` (CPUMetrics), `history` [CPUMetrics]
- `MemoryMonitor`: `current` (MemoryMetrics), `history` [MemoryMetrics]
- `NetworkMonitor`: `current` (NetworkMetrics), `history` [NetworkMetrics]
- `BatteryMonitor`: `current` (BatteryMetrics), `history` [BatteryMetrics]

These could be used to generate sparklines if the settings were implemented.

### Summary Table

| Aspect | Status | Location |
|--------|--------|----------|
| **Network speeds (↓/↑)** | Actively rendered | MenuBarController.swift:40-44 |
| **Battery info** | Actively rendered | MenuBarController.swift:46-58 |
| **Sparklines** | Settings exist but NOT rendered | Settings defined, SparklineRenderer available, but not integrated |
| **CPU/Memory text** | No | Not displayed |
| **Update frequency** | Every 1 second | MenuBarController.swift:15 |
| **Font** | Monospaced, 11pt, white | MenuBarController.swift:36 |

The menu bar is currently a **minimal network/battery indicator** rather than a comprehensive system monitor dashboard.

1→import Foundation
     2→
     3→final class AlertRulesEngine {
     4→    enum Rule: String, CaseIterable {
     5→        case cpuUsageHigh
     6→        case cpuTempHigh
     7→        case memoryPressure
     8→        case packetLoss
     9→        case highJitter
    10→        case lowDisk
    11→        case lowBattery
    12→        case networkIncident
    13→    }
    14→
    15→    private let monitors: MonitorCoordinator
    16→    private let historyStore: HistoryStore
    17→    private let settings: AppSettings
    18→    private var timer: Timer?
    19→    private var lastTriggeredAt: [Rule: Date] = [:]
    20→    private var lastIncidentType: NetworkIncidentType?
    21→    private var lastPresentedAlertAt: Date?
    22→
    23→    var onAlert: ((TimelineSeverity, String, String) -> Void)?
    24→
    25→    init(
    26→        monitors: MonitorCoordinator,
    27→        historyStore: HistoryStore,
    28→        settings: AppSettings = .shared
    29→    ) {
    30→        self.monitors = monitors
    31→        self.historyStore = historyStore
    32→        self.settings = settings
    33→    }
    34→
    35→    func start() {
    36→        timer?.invalidate()
    37→        timer = Timer.scheduledTimer(withTimeInterval: 15, repeats: true) { [weak self] _ in
    38→            self?.evaluateRules()
    39→        }
    40→        evaluateRules()
    41→    }
    42→
    43→    func stop() {
    44→        timer?.invalidate()
    45→        timer = nil
    46→    }
    47→
    48→    func evaluateRules() {
    49→        evaluateIncidentState()
    50→        guard settings.alertsEnabled else { return }
    51→
    52→        if let cpu = monitors.cpu.current {
    53→            if cpu.totalUsage >= settings.cpuUsageAlertThreshold {
    54→                trigger(
    55→                    .cpuUsageHigh,
    56→                    severity: .warning,
    57→                    title: "High CPU Usage",
    58→                    message: String(format: "CPU usage is %.0f%%.", cpu.totalUsage)
    59→                )
    60→            }
    61→
    62→            if let temp = cpu.temperature, temp >= settings.cpuTempAlertThreshold {
    63→                trigger(
    64→                    .cpuTempHigh,
    65→                    severity: .critical,
    66→                    title: "High CPU Temperature",
    67→                    message: String(format: "CPU temperature is %.0f°C.", temp)
    68→                )
    69→            }
    70→        }
    71→
    72→        if let memory = monitors.memory.current, memory.pressure == .critical {
    73→            trigger(
    74→                .memoryPressure,
    75→                severity: .critical,
    76→                title: "Critical Memory Pressure",
    77→                message: "Memory pressure is critical. Close heavy apps."
    78→            )
    79→        }
    80→
    81→        if let wifi = monitors.wifi.current {
    82→            let packetLoss = max(wifi.routerPacketLoss ?? 0, wifi.internetPacketLoss ?? 0)
    83→            if packetLoss >= settings.packetLossAlertThreshold {
    84→                trigger(
    85→                    .packetLoss,
    86→                    severity: .warning,
    87→                    title: "Packet Loss Detected",
    88→                    message: String(format: "Packet loss is %.1f%%.", packetLoss)
    89→                )
    90→            }
    91→
    92→            let jitter = max(wifi.routerJitter ?? 0, wifi.internetJitter ?? 0)
    93→            if jitter >= settings.jitterAlertThreshold {
    94→                trigger(
    95→                    .highJitter,
    96→                    severity: .warning,
    97→                    title: "High Jitter Detected",
    98→                    message: String(format: "Jitter is %.0f ms.", jitter)
    99→                )
   100→            }
   101→        }
   102→
   103→        if let firstVolume = monitors.disk.current?.volumes.first {
   104→            let freePercent = firstVolume.total > 0 ? (Double(firstVolume.free) / Double(firstVolume.total) * 100) : 100
   105→            if freePercent <= settings.lowDiskFreePercentThreshold {
   106→                trigger(
   107→                    .lowDisk,
   108→                    severity: .critical,
   109→                    title: "Low Disk Space",
   110→                    message: String(format: "%@ has %.1f%% free space.", firstVolume.name, freePercent)
   111→                )
   112→            }
   113→        }
   114→
   115→        if settings.lowBatteryAlertsEnabled,
   116→           let battery = monitors.battery.current,
   117→           battery.powerSource == .battery,
   118→           !battery.isCharging,
   119→           battery.chargePercent <= settings.lowBatteryAlertThreshold {
   120→            trigger(
   121→                .lowBattery,
   122→                severity: .critical,
   123→                title: "Low Battery",
   124→                message: String(format: "Battery is at %.0f%%.", battery.chargePercent)
   125→            )
   126→        }
   127→    }
   128→
   129→    private func evaluateIncidentState() {
   130→        let incident = NetworkIncidentAnalyzer.detect(from: monitors.wifi.current)
   131→        let incidentType = incident?.type
   132→
   133→        if incidentType != lastIncidentType {
   134→            if let incident {
   135→                historyStore.recordEvent(TimelineEvent(
   136→                    severity: .warning,
   137→                    category: .network,
   138→                    title: incident.title,
   139→                    message: incident.hint
   140→                ))
   141→
   142→                trigger(
   143→                    .networkIncident,
   144→                    severity: .warning,
   145→                    title: incident.title,
   146→                    message: incident.hint
   147→                )
   148→            } else if lastIncidentType != nil {
   149→                historyStore.recordEvent(TimelineEvent(
   150→                    severity: .info,
   151→                    category: .network,
   152→                    title: "Network Recovered",
   153→                    message: "Connectivity checks are back to normal."
   154→                ))
   155→            }
   156→            lastIncidentType = incidentType
   157→        }
   158→    }
   159→
   160→    private func trigger(
   161→        _ rule: Rule,
   162→        severity: TimelineSeverity,
   163→        title: String,
   164→        message: String
   165→    ) {
   166→        let now = Date()
   167→        let cooldown = settings.alertsCooldownMinutes * 60
   168→        if let last = lastTriggeredAt[rule], now.timeIntervalSince(last) < cooldown {
   169→            return
   170→        }
   171→
   172→        lastTriggeredAt[rule] = now
   173→        historyStore.recordEvent(TimelineEvent(
   174→            severity: severity,
   175→            category: category(for: rule),
   176→            title: title,
   177→            message: message
   178→        ))
   179→
   180→        guard !isInQuietHours() else { return }
   181→
   182→        // Keep a small global spacing between popups from different rules.
   183→        if let lastPresentedAlertAt, now.timeIntervalSince(lastPresentedAlertAt) < 5 {
   184→            return
   185→        }
   186→
   187→        lastPresentedAlertAt = now
   188→        onAlert?(severity, title, message)
   189→    }
   190→
   191→    private func category(for rule: Rule) -> TimelineCategory {
   192→        switch rule {
   193→        case .cpuUsageHigh, .cpuTempHigh, .memoryPressure:
   194→            return .alert
   195→        case .packetLoss, .highJitter, .networkIncident:
   196→            return .network
   197→        case .lowDisk:
   198→            return .disk
   199→        case .lowBattery:
   200→            return .battery
   201→        }
   202→    }
   203→
   204→    private func isInQuietHours() -> Bool {
   205→        guard settings.alertsQuietHoursEnabled else { return false }
   206→
   207→        let calendar = Calendar.current
   208→        let hour = calendar.component(.hour, from: Date())
   209→        let start = Int(settings.alertsQuietHoursStartHour)
   210→        let end = Int(settings.alertsQuietHoursEndHour)
   211→
   212→        if start == end {
   213→            return true
   214→        }
   215→
   216→        if start < end {
   217→            return hour >= start && hour < end
   218→        }
   219→
   220→        // Overnight interval (e.g. 22 -> 7)
   221→        return hour >= start || hour < end
   222→    }
   223→}

1→import Foundation
     2→
     3→@Observable
     4→class CPUMonitor {
     5→    var current: CPUMetrics?
     6→    var history: [CPUMetrics] = []
     7→
     8→    private var timer: Timer?
     9→    private var previousInfo: host_cpu_load_info_data_t?
    10→    private var previousPerCoreTicks: [CPUTicks] = []
    11→    private var smcReader: SMCReader?
    12→
    13→    private struct CPUTicks {
    14→        let user: Int64
    15→        let system: Int64
    16→        let idle: Int64
    17→        let nice: Int64
    18→    }
    19→
    20→    func start(interval: TimeInterval = 1.0) {
    21→        smcReader = SMCReader()
    22→        timer?.invalidate()
    23→        previousInfo = nil
    24→        previousPerCoreTicks = []
    25→        timer = Timer.scheduledTimer(withTimeInterval: interval, repeats: true) { [weak self] _ in
    26→            self?.update()
    27→        }
    28→        update()
    29→    }
    30→
    31→    func stop() {
    32→        timer?.invalidate()
    33→        timer = nil
    34→    }
    35→
    36→    private func update() {
    37→        guard let metrics = fetchMetrics() else { return }
    38→
    39→        current = metrics
    40→        history.append(metrics)
    41→
    42→        if history.count > 120 {
    43→            history.removeFirst()
    44→        }
    45→    }
    46→
    47→    private func fetchMetrics() -> CPUMetrics? {
    48→        var cpuLoad = host_cpu_load_info()
    49→        var count = mach_msg_type_number_t(MemoryLayout<host_cpu_load_info>.stride / MemoryLayout<integer_t>.stride)
    50→
    51→        let result = withUnsafeMutablePointer(to: &cpuLoad) {
    52→            $0.withMemoryRebound(to: integer_t.self, capacity: Int(count)) { pointer in
    53→                host_statistics(mach_host_self(), HOST_CPU_LOAD_INFO, pointer, &count)
    54→            }
    55→        }
    56→
    57→        guard result == KERN_SUCCESS else { return nil }
    58→
    59→        let userTime = Int64(cpuLoad.cpu_ticks.0)
    60→        let systemTime = Int64(cpuLoad.cpu_ticks.1)
    61→        let idleTime = Int64(cpuLoad.cpu_ticks.2)
    62→        let niceTime = Int64(cpuLoad.cpu_ticks.3)
    63→        let currentTicks = CPUTicks(user: userTime, system: systemTime, idle: idleTime, nice: niceTime)
    64→        let totalUsage: Double
    65→
    66→        if let previousInfo {
    67→            let previousTicks = CPUTicks(
    68→                user: Int64(previousInfo.cpu_ticks.0),
    69→                system: Int64(previousInfo.cpu_ticks.1),
    70→                idle: Int64(previousInfo.cpu_ticks.2),
    71→                nice: Int64(previousInfo.cpu_ticks.3)
    72→            )
    73→            totalUsage = usagePercentage(from: previousTicks, to: currentTicks)
    74→        } else {
    75→            totalUsage = 0
    76→        }
    77→        previousInfo = cpuLoad
    78→
    79→        let measuredPerCoreUsage = fetchPerCoreUsage()
    80→        let hasPerCoreSignal = measuredPerCoreUsage.contains { $0 > 0.05 }
    81→        let perCoreUsage = hasPerCoreSignal
    82→            ? measuredPerCoreUsage
    83→            : fallbackPerCoreUsage(totalUsage: totalUsage)
    84→        let resolvedTotalUsage = perCoreUsage.isEmpty
    85→            ? totalUsage
    86→            : (perCoreUsage.reduce(0, +) / Double(perCoreUsage.count))
    87→
    88→        return CPUMetrics(
    89→            timestamp: Date(),
    90→            totalUsage: min(max(resolvedTotalUsage, 0), 100),
    91→            perCoreUsage: perCoreUsage,
    92→            temperature: fetchTemperature(),
    93→            frequency: fetchFrequency(),
    94→            isThrottled: false
    95→        )
    96→    }
    97→
    98→    private func fetchPerCoreUsage() -> [Double] {
    99→        var cpuInfo: processor_info_array_t?
   100→        var cpuInfoCount: mach_msg_type_number_t = 0
   101→        var cpuCount: natural_t = 0
   102→
   103→        let result = host_processor_info(
   104→            mach_host_self(),
   105→            PROCESSOR_CPU_LOAD_INFO,
   106→            &cpuCount,
   107→            &cpuInfo,
   108→            &cpuInfoCount
   109→        )
   110→
   111→        guard result == KERN_SUCCESS, let cpuInfo else {
   112→            return previousPerCoreTicks.map { _ in 0 }
   113→        }
   114→
   115→        let cpuStateCount = Int(CPU_STATE_MAX)
   116→        var currentPerCoreTicks: [CPUTicks] = []
   117→        currentPerCoreTicks.reserveCapacity(Int(cpuCount))
   118→
   119→        for core in 0..<Int(cpuCount) {
   120→            let base = core * cpuStateCount
   121→            let user = Int64(cpuInfo[base + Int(CPU_STATE_USER)])
   122→            let system = Int64(cpuInfo[base + Int(CPU_STATE_SYSTEM)])
   123→            let idle = Int64(cpuInfo[base + Int(CPU_STATE_IDLE)])
   124→            let nice = Int64(cpuInfo[base + Int(CPU_STATE_NICE)])
   125→            currentPerCoreTicks.append(CPUTicks(user: user, system: system, idle: idle, nice: nice))
   126→        }
   127→
   128→        let deallocateSize = vm_size_t(Int(cpuInfoCount) * MemoryLayout<integer_t>.stride)
   129→        vm_deallocate(mach_task_self_, vm_address_t(bitPattern: cpuInfo), deallocateSize)
   130→
   131→        guard previousPerCoreTicks.count == currentPerCoreTicks.count else {
   132→            previousPerCoreTicks = currentPerCoreTicks
   133→            return currentPerCoreTicks.map { _ in 0 }
   134→        }
   135→
   136→        let usages = zip(previousPerCoreTicks, currentPerCoreTicks).map { previousTicks, currentTicks in
   137→            usagePercentage(from: previousTicks, to: currentTicks)
   138→        }
   139→        previousPerCoreTicks = currentPerCoreTicks
   140→        return usages
   141→    }
   142→
   143→    private func usagePercentage(from previous: CPUTicks, to current: CPUTicks) -> Double {
   144→        let userDelta = safeDelta(current.user, previous.user)
   145→        let systemDelta = safeDelta(current.system, previous.system)
   146→        let idleDelta = safeDelta(current.idle, previous.idle)
   147→        let niceDelta = safeDelta(current.nice, previous.nice)
   148→
   149→        let totalDelta = userDelta + systemDelta + idleDelta + niceDelta
   150→        guard totalDelta > 0 else { return 0 }
   151→
   152→        let usedDelta = userDelta + systemDelta + niceDelta
   153→        return (Double(usedDelta) / Double(totalDelta)) * 100.0
   154→    }
   155→
   156→    private func safeDelta(_ current: Int64, _ previous: Int64) -> UInt64 {
   157→        guard current >= previous else {
   158→            return UInt64(max(current, 0))
   159→        }
   160→        return UInt64(current - previous)
   161→    }
   162→
   163→    private func fetchTemperature() -> Double? {
   164→        return smcReader?.getCPUTemperature()
   165→    }
   166→
   167→    private func fetchFrequency() -> Double? {
   168→        // Would use sysctl to get current CPU frequency
   169→        return 3.2
   170→    }
   171→
   172→    private func fallbackPerCoreUsage(totalUsage: Double) -> [Double] {
   173→        let coreCount = max(ProcessInfo.processInfo.activeProcessorCount, 1)
   174→        let clamped = min(max(totalUsage, 0), 100)
   175→        return Array(repeating: clamped, count: coreCount)
   176→    }
   177→}

     4→class MemoryMonitor {
     5→    var current: MemoryMetrics?
     6→    var history: [MemoryMetrics] = []
     7→
     8→    private var timer: Timer?
     9→
    10→    func start(interval: TimeInterval = 2.0) {
    11→        timer?.invalidate()
    12→        timer = Timer.scheduledTimer(withTimeInterval: interval, repeats: true) { [weak self] _ in
    13→            self?.update()
    14→        }
    15→        update()
    16→    }
    17→
    18→    func stop() {
    19→        timer?.invalidate()
    20→        timer = nil
    21→    }
    22→
    23→    private func update() {
    24→        guard let metrics = fetchMetrics() else { return }
    25→
    26→        current = metrics
    27→        history.append(metrics)
    28→
    29→        if history.count > 120 {
    30→            history.removeFirst()
    31→        }
    32→    }
    33→
    34→    private func fetchMetrics() -> MemoryMetrics? {
    35→        var stats = vm_statistics64()
    36→        var count = mach_msg_type_number_t(MemoryLayout<vm_statistics64>.stride / MemoryLayout<integer_t>.stride)
    37→
    38→        let result = withUnsafeMutablePointer(to: &stats) {
    39→            $0.withMemoryRebound(to: integer_t.self, capacity: Int(count)) { pointer in
    40→                host_statistics64(mach_host_self(), HOST_VM_INFO64, pointer, &count)
    41→            }
    42→        }
    43→
    44→        guard result == KERN_SUCCESS else { return nil }
    45→
    46→        let pageSize = UInt64(vm_kernel_page_size)
    47→        let active = UInt64(stats.active_count) * pageSize
    48→        let inactive = UInt64(stats.inactive_count) * pageSize
    49→        let wired = UInt64(stats.wire_count) * pageSize
    50→        let compressed = UInt64(stats.compressor_page_count) * pageSize
    51→        let free = UInt64(stats.free_count) * pageSize
    52→
    53→        let used = active + inactive
    54→
    55→        var size: UInt64 = 0
    56→        var sizeLength = MemoryLayout<UInt64>.size
    57→        sysctlbyname("hw.memsize", &size, &sizeLength, nil, 0)
    58→
    59→        let pressure: MemoryPressure
    60→        let usedPercentage = Double(used + wired) / Double(size)
    61→        if usedPercentage > 0.9 {
    62→            pressure = .critical
    63→        } else if usedPercentage > 0.7 {
    64→            pressure = .warning
    65→        } else {
    66→            pressure = .normal
    67→        }
    68→
    69→        return MemoryMetrics(
    70→            timestamp: Date(),
    71→            used: used,
    72→            wired: wired,
    73→            compressed: compressed,
    74→            free: free,
    75→            total: size,
    76→            pressure: pressure,
    77→            swapUsed: UInt64(stats.swapins) * pageSize,
    78→            swapTotal: UInt64(stats.swapins + stats.swapouts) * pageSize
    79→        )
    80→    }
    81→}

1→import Foundation
     2→import Network
     3→
     4→@Observable
     5→class NetworkMonitor {
     6→    var current: NetworkMetrics?
     7→    var history: [NetworkMetrics] = []
     8→
     9→    private var timer: Timer?
    10→    private var previousBytesIn: UInt64 = 0
    11→    private var previousBytesOut: UInt64 = 0
    12→    private var previousSampleAt: Date?
    13→    private var pathMonitor: NWPathMonitor?
    14→    private var cachedActiveConnections = 0
    15→    private var lastConnectionCountRefresh = Date.distantPast
    16→
    17→    func start(interval: TimeInterval = 1.0) {
    18→        timer?.invalidate()
    19→        previousBytesIn = 0
    20→        previousBytesOut = 0
    21→        previousSampleAt = nil
    22→        pathMonitor = NWPathMonitor()
    23→        pathMonitor?.start(queue: DispatchQueue.global(qos: .background))
    24→
    25→        timer = Timer.scheduledTimer(withTimeInterval: interval, repeats: true) { [weak self] _ in
    26→            self?.update()
    27→        }
    28→        update()
    29→    }
    30→
    31→    func stop() {
    32→        timer?.invalidate()
    33→        timer = nil
    34→        pathMonitor?.cancel()
    35→        pathMonitor = nil
    36→    }
    37→
    38→    private func update() {
    39→        guard let metrics = fetchMetrics() else { return }
    40→
    41→        current = metrics
    42→        history.append(metrics)
    43→
    44→        if history.count > 120 {
    45→            history.removeFirst()
    46→        }
    47→    }
    48→
    49→    private func fetchMetrics() -> NetworkMetrics? {
    50→        let now = Date()
    51→        var bytesIn: UInt64 = 0
    52→        var bytesOut: UInt64 = 0
    53→        var interfaceName = "en0"
    54→
    55→        if let addrs = getifaddrs_wrapper() {
    56→            var ptr: UnsafeMutablePointer<ifaddrs>? = addrs
    57→            while let current = ptr {
    58→                defer { ptr = current.pointee.ifa_next }
    59→
    60→                let interface = current.pointee
    61→                let name = String(cString: interface.ifa_name)
    62→
    63→                if name.starts(with: "en"), let data = interface.ifa_data {
    64→                    let networkData = data.assumingMemoryBound(to: if_data.self).pointee
    65→                    bytesIn = saturatingAdd(bytesIn, UInt64(networkData.ifi_ibytes))
    66→                    bytesOut = saturatingAdd(bytesOut, UInt64(networkData.ifi_obytes))
    67→                    interfaceName = name
    68→                }
    69→            }
    70→            freeifaddrs(addrs)
    71→        }
    72→
    73→        let elapsedSeconds: Double
    74→        if let previousSampleAt {
    75→            elapsedSeconds = max(now.timeIntervalSince(previousSampleAt), 0.001)
    76→        } else {
    77→            elapsedSeconds = 1.0
    78→        }
    79→
    80→        let uploadDelta = counterDelta(current: bytesOut, previous: previousBytesOut)
    81→        let downloadDelta = counterDelta(current: bytesIn, previous: previousBytesIn)
    82→        let uploadSpeed = previousSampleAt == nil ? 0 : Double(uploadDelta) / elapsedSeconds
    83→        let downloadSpeed = previousSampleAt == nil ? 0 : Double(downloadDelta) / elapsedSeconds
    84→
    85→        previousBytesIn = bytesIn
    86→        previousBytesOut = bytesOut
    87→        previousSampleAt = now
    88→
    89→        return NetworkMetrics(
    90→            timestamp: now,
    91→            uploadSpeed: uploadSpeed,
    92→            downloadSpeed: downloadSpeed,
    93→            interfaceName: interfaceName,
    94→            localIP: getLocalIP(),
    95→            publicIP: nil, // Fetched async
    96→            activeConnections: getActiveConnections()
    97→        )
    98→    }
    99→
   100→    private func counterDelta(current: UInt64, previous: UInt64) -> UInt64 {
   101→        guard previous > 0 else { return 0 }
   102→
   103→        if current >= previous {
   104→            return current - previous
   105→        }
   106→
   107→        // Interface counters can reset across reconnects or interface swaps.
   108→        // Treat as a restart to avoid overflow and unrealistic spikes.
   109→        return current
   110→    }
   111→
   112→    private func saturatingAdd(_ lhs: UInt64, _ rhs: UInt64) -> UInt64 {
   113→        let (value, overflowed) = lhs.addingReportingOverflow(rhs)
   114→        return overflowed ? UInt64.max : value
   115→    }
   116→
   117→    private func getifaddrs_wrapper() -> UnsafeMutablePointer<ifaddrs>? {
   118→        var addrs: UnsafeMutablePointer<ifaddrs>?
   119→        guard getifaddrs(&addrs) == 0 else { return nil }
   120→        return addrs
   121→    }
   122→
   123→    private func getLocalIP() -> String? {
   124→        var address: String?
   125→        var ifaddr: UnsafeMutablePointer<ifaddrs>?
   126→
   127→        if getifaddrs(&ifaddr) == 0 {
   128→            var ptr = ifaddr
   129→            while ptr != nil {
   130→                defer { ptr = ptr?.pointee.ifa_next }
   131→
   132→                let interface = ptr?.pointee
   133→                let addrFamily = interface?.ifa_addr.pointee.sa_family
   134→
   135→                if addrFamily == UInt8(AF_INET) {
   136→                    let name = String(cString: (interface?.ifa_name)!)
   137→                    if name == "en0" || name == "en1" {
   138→                        var hostname = [CChar](repeating: 0, count: Int(NI_MAXHOST))
   139→                        getnameinfo(interface?.ifa_addr, socklen_t((interface?.ifa_addr.pointee.sa_len)!),
   140→                                    &hostname, socklen_t(hostname.count),
   141→                                    nil, socklen_t(0), NI_NUMERICHOST)
   142→                        address = String(cString: hostname)
   143→                    }
   144→                }
   145→            }
   146→            freeifaddrs(ifaddr)
   147→        }
   148→        return address
   149→    }
   150→
   151→    private func getActiveConnections() -> Int {
   152→        let now = Date()
   153→        if now.timeIntervalSince(lastConnectionCountRefresh) < 5 {
   154→            return cachedActiveConnections
   155→        }
   156→
   157→        lastConnectionCountRefresh = now
   158→        cachedActiveConnections = fetchActiveConnectionsViaNetstat() ?? cachedActiveConnections
   159→        return cachedActiveConnections
   160→    }
   161→
   162→    private func fetchActiveConnectionsViaNetstat() -> Int? {
   163→        let tcpOutput = runCommand("/usr/sbin/netstat", arguments: ["-an", "-p", "tcp"]) ?? ""
   164→        let udpOutput = runCommand("/usr/sbin/netstat", arguments: ["-an", "-p", "udp"]) ?? ""
   165→
   166→        guard !tcpOutput.isEmpty || !udpOutput.isEmpty else { return nil }
   167→
   168→        let tcpCount = tcpOutput
   169→            .split(whereSeparator: \.isNewline)
   170→            .filter { line in
   171→                let text = line.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespaces)
   172→                guard text.hasPrefix("tcp") else { return false }
   173→
   174→                let cols = text.split(whereSeparator: \.isWhitespace)
   175→                guard let state = cols.last.map(String.init) else { return false }
   176→                return state != "LISTEN" && state != "CLOSED"
   177→            }
   178→            .count
   179→
   180→        let udpCount = udpOutput
   181→            .split(whereSeparator: \.isNewline)
   182→            .filter { line in
   183→                let text = line.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespaces)
   184→                return text.hasPrefix("udp")
   185→            }
   186→            .count
   187→
   188→        return tcpCount + udpCount
   189→    }
   190→
   191→    private func runCommand(_ executable: String, arguments: [String]) -> String? {
   192→        let process = Process()
   193→        let pipe = Pipe()
   194→        let errorPipe = Pipe()
   195→
   196→        process.executableURL = URL(fileURLWithPath: executable)
   197→        process.arguments = arguments
   198→        process.standardOutput = pipe
   199→        process.standardError = errorPipe
   200→
   201→        do {
   202→            try process.run()
   203→        } catch {
   204→            return nil
   205→        }
   206→
   207→        let data = pipe.fileHandleForReading.readDataToEndOfFile()
   208→        process.waitUntilExit()
   209→
   210→        guard process.terminationStatus == 0 else { return nil }
   211→        return String(data: data, encoding: .utf8)
   212→    }
   213→}
   214→
