    77→        cleanPaths(targets, title: category.title, category: category)
    78→    }
    79→
    80→    func revealCategory(_ category: DiskCleanupCategoryKey) {
    81→        let homeURL = FileManager.default.homeDirectoryForCurrentUser
    82→        let targets = categoryPaths(for: category, homeURL: homeURL)
    83→
    84→        guard let firstExisting = targets.first(where: {
    85→            FileManager.default.fileExists(atPath: URL(fileURLWithPath: $0).path)
    86→        }) else {
    87→            cleanupStatus = "No path found for \(category.title)."
    88→            return
    89→        }
    90→
    91→        NSWorkspace.shared.activateFileViewerSelecting([URL(fileURLWithPath: firstExisting)])
    92→    }
    93→
    94→    func moveItemToTrash(_ consumer: DiskSpaceConsumer) {
    95→        guard !isCleaningUpSpace else { return }
    96→
    97→        let homePath = FileManager.default.homeDirectoryForCurrentUser.path
    98→        guard consumer.path.hasPrefix(homePath + "/") else {
    99→            cleanupStatus = "Only items in your home directory can be moved to Trash."
   100→            return
   101→        }
   102→
   103→        isCleaningUpSpace = true
   104→        cleanupStatus = nil
   105→
   106→        let sourceURL = URL(fileURLWithPath: consumer.path)
   107→        DispatchQueue.global(qos: .utility).async { [weak self] in
   108→            guard let self else { return }
   109→
   110→            do {
   111→                _ = try FileManager.default.trashItem(at: sourceURL, resultingItemURL: nil)
   112→
   113→                DispatchQueue.main.async { [weak self] in
   114→                    guard let self else { return }
   115→                    self.cleanupStatus = "Moved \(consumer.name) to Trash (\(self.formatBytes(consumer.size)))."
   116→                    self.historyStore.recordEvent(TimelineEvent(
   117→                        severity: .info,
   118→                        category: .disk,
   119→                        title: "Item Moved to Trash",
   120→                        message: "\(consumer.name) (\(self.formatBytes(consumer.size)))."
   121→                    ))
   122→                    self.isCleaningUpSpace = false
   123→                    self.refreshSpaceAnalysis()
   124→                }
   125→            } catch {
   126→                DispatchQueue.main.async { [weak self] in
   127→                    guard let self else { return }
   128→                    self.cleanupStatus = "Failed to move \(consumer.name) to Trash: \(error.localizedDescription)"
   129→                    self.isCleaningUpSpace = false
   130→                }
   131→            }
   132→        }
   133→    }
   134→
   135→    private func cleanPaths(_ paths: [String], title: String, category: DiskCleanupCategoryKey) {
   136→        guard !paths.isEmpty else {
   137→            cleanupStatus = "No paths found for \(title)."
   138→            return
   139→        }
   140→
   141→        let homePath = FileManager.default.homeDirectoryForCurrentUser.path
   142→        let safePaths = paths.filter { $0.hasPrefix(homePath + "/") }
   143→        guard !safePaths.isEmpty else {
   144→            cleanupStatus = "Cleanup is limited to paths in your home directory."
   145→            return
   146→        }
   147→
   148→        isCleaningUpSpace = true
   149→        cleanupStatus = nil
   150→
   151→        let estimatedRemovedSize = safePaths.reduce(UInt64(0)) { partial, path in
   152→            partial + sizeOfItem(at: URL(fileURLWithPath: path))
   153→        }
   154→
   155→        DispatchQueue.global(qos: .utility).async { [weak self] in
   156→            guard let self else { return }
   157→            let fileManager = FileManager.default
   158→
   159→            var removedItems = 0
   160→            var failedItems = 0
   161→
   162→            for path in safePaths {
   163→                let url = URL(fileURLWithPath: path)
   164→                var isDirectory: ObjCBool = false
   165→                guard fileManager.fileExists(atPath: url.path, isDirectory: &isDirectory) else { continue }
   166→
   167→                if isDirectory.boolValue {
   168→                    do {
   169→                        let children = try fileManager.contentsOfDirectory(at: url, includingPropertiesForKeys: nil, options: [])
   170→                        for child in children {
   171→                            do {
   172→                                try fileManager.removeItem(at: child)
   173→                                removedItems += 1
   174→                            } catch {
   175→                                failedItems += 1
   176→                            }
   177→                        }
   178→                    } catch {
   179→                        failedItems += 1
   180→                    }
   181→                } else {
   182→                    do {
   183→                        try fileManager.removeItem(at: url)
   184→                        removedItems += 1
   185→                    } catch {
   186→                        failedItems += 1
   187→                    }
   188→                }
   189→            }
   190→
   191→            DispatchQueue.main.async { [weak self] in
   192→                guard let self else { return }
   193→                let freedText = self.formatBytes(estimatedRemovedSize)
   194→                if failedItems == 0 {
   195→                    self.cleanupStatus = "Cleaned \(title) (\(freedText), \(removedItems) items)."
   196→                } else {
   197→                    self.cleanupStatus = "Cleaned \(title) (\(freedText), \(removedItems) items, \(failedItems) failed)."
   198→                }
   199→
   200→                self.historyStore.recordEvent(TimelineEvent(
   201→                    severity: failedItems == 0 ? .info : .warning,
   202→                    category: .disk,
   203→                    title: "Cleanup: \(category.title)",
   204→                    message: self.cleanupStatus ?? "Cleanup completed."
   205→                ))
   206→
   207→                self.isCleaningUpSpace = false
   208→                self.refreshSpaceAnalysis()
   209→            }
   210→        }
   211→    }
   212→
   213→    private func update() {
   214→        guard let metrics = fetchMetrics() else { return }
   215→
   216→        current = metrics
   217→        history.append(metrics)
   218→
   219→        if history.count > 120 {
   220→            history.removeFirst()
   221→        }
   222→    }
   223→
   224→    private func fetchMetrics() -> DiskMetrics? {
   225→        let sampleTime = Date()
   226→        let volumes = FileManager.default.mountedVolumeURLs(includingResourceValuesForKeys: [
   227→            .volumeNameKey,
   228→            .volumeTotalCapacityKey,
   229→            .volumeAvailableCapacityKey
   230→        ], options: [.skipHiddenVolumes])?.compactMap { url -> VolumeInfo? in
   231→            guard let values = try? url.resourceValues(forKeys: [
   232→                .volumeNameKey,
   233→                .volumeTotalCapacityKey,
   234→                .volumeAvailableCapacityKey
   235→            ]) else { return nil }
   236→
   237→            guard let total = values.volumeTotalCapacity,
   238→                  let available = values.volumeAvailableCapacity,
   239→                  let name = values.volumeName else { return nil }
   240→
   241→            let used = UInt64(total - available)
   242→
   243→            return VolumeInfo(
   244→                name: name,
   245→                path: url.path,
   246→                total: UInt64(total),
   247→                used: used,
   248→                free: UInt64(available),
   249→                smartStatus: .verified
   250→            )
   251→        } ?? []
   252→
   253→        let counters = readDiskIOCounters()
   254→        let elapsed = previousIOSampleTime.map { sampleTime.timeIntervalSince($0) } ?? 0
   255→
   256→        let readSpeed: Double
   257→        let writeSpeed: Double
   258→        if let counters, elapsed > 0 {
   259→            let readDelta = counters.read >= previousReadBytes ? counters.read - previousReadBytes : 0
   260→            let writeDelta = counters.write >= previousWriteBytes ? counters.write - previousWriteBytes : 0
   261→            readSpeed = Double(readDelta) / elapsed
   262→            writeSpeed = Double(writeDelta) / elapsed
   263→            previousReadBytes = counters.read
   264→            previousWriteBytes = counters.write
   265→        } else {
   266→            readSpeed = 0
   267→            writeSpeed = 0
   268→            if let counters {
   269→                previousReadBytes = counters.read
   270→                previousWriteBytes = counters.write
   271→            }
   272→        }
   273→        previousIOSampleTime = sampleTime
   274→
   275→        return DiskMetrics(
   276→            timestamp: sampleTime,
   277→            volumes: volumes,
   278→            readSpeed: readSpeed,
   279→            writeSpeed: writeSpeed
   280→        )
   281→    }
   282→
   283→    private func buildSpaceAnalysis(homeURL: URL) -> SpaceAnalysisResult {
   284→        let fileManager = FileManager.default
   285→        let candidateRelativePaths = [
   286→            "Library/Caches",
   287→            "Library/Application Support",
   288→            "Library/Containers",
   289→            "Library/Group Containers",
   290→            "Downloads",
   291→            "Documents",
   292→            "Desktop",
   293→            "Movies",
   294→            "Pictures",
   295→            "Developer",
   296→            ".Trash"
   297→        ]
   298→
   299→        var consumers: [DiskSpaceConsumer] = []
   300→        let cacheURL = homeURL.appendingPathComponent("Library/Caches", isDirectory: true)
   301→        let cacheSize = sizeOfItem(at: cacheURL)
   302→
   303→        for relativePath in candidateRelativePaths {
   304→            let url = homeURL.appendingPathComponent(relativePath, isDirectory: true)
   305→            var isDirectory: ObjCBool = false
   306→            guard fileManager.fileExists(atPath: url.path, isDirectory: &isDirectory), isDirectory.boolValue else {
   307→                continue
   308→            }
   309→
   310→            let size = sizeOfItem(at: url)
   311→            guard size > 0 else { continue }
   312→
   313→            consumers.append(DiskSpaceConsumer(
   314→                name: relativePath,
   315→                path: url.path,
   316→                size: size
   317→            ))
   318→        }
   319→
   320→        consumers.sort { $0.size > $1.size }
   321→        let topConsumers = Array(consumers.prefix(8))
   322→
   323→        let cacheEntries = largestChildren(in: cacheURL, maxCount: 6)
   324→        let cleanupCategories = buildCleanupCategories(homeURL: homeURL)
   325→
   326→        return SpaceAnalysisResult(
   327→            cacheFolderSize: cacheSize,
   328→            cleanupCategories: cleanupCategories,
   329→            largestConsumers: topConsumers,
   330→            largestCacheEntries: cacheEntries
   331→        )
   332→    }
   333→
   334→    private func buildCleanupCategories(homeURL: URL) -> [DiskCleanupCategoryStatus] {
   335→        DiskCleanupCategoryKey.allCases.map { key in
   336→            let paths = categoryPaths(for: key, homeURL: homeURL).filter {
   337→                FileManager.default.fileExists(atPath: $0)
   338→            }
   339→            let size = paths.reduce(UInt64(0)) { partial, path in
   340→                partial + sizeOfItem(at: URL(fileURLWithPath: path))
   341→            }
   342→            return DiskCleanupCategoryStatus(key: key, paths: paths, size: size)
   343→        }
   344→    }
   345→
   346→    private func categoryPaths(for category: DiskCleanupCategoryKey, homeURL: URL) -> [String] {
   347→        switch category {
   348→        case .caches:
   349→            return [homeURL.appendingPathComponent("Library/Caches", isDirectory: true).path]
   350→        case .logs:
   351→            return [homeURL.appendingPathComponent("Library/Logs", isDirectory: true).path]
   352→        case .derivedData:
   353→            return [homeURL.appendingPathComponent("Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData", isDirectory: true).path]
   354→        case .browserCaches:
   355→            return [
   356→                homeURL.appendingPathComponent("Library/Caches/com.apple.Safari", isDirectory: true).path,
   357→                homeURL.appendingPathComponent("Library/Caches/Google/Chrome", isDirectory: true).path,
   358→                homeURL.appendingPathComponent("Library/Caches/Firefox", isDirectory: true).path,
   359→                homeURL.appendingPathComponent("Library/Caches/Microsoft Edge", isDirectory: true).path,
   360→                homeURL.appendingPathComponent("Library/Caches/com.brave.Browser", isDirectory: true).path,
   361→                homeURL.appendingPathComponent("Library/Application Support/Firefox/Profiles", isDirectory: true).path
   362→            ]
   363→        }
   364→    }
   365→
   366→    private func largestChildren(in directoryURL: URL, maxCount: Int) -> [DiskSpaceConsumer] {
   367→        let fileManager = FileManager.default
   368→        guard let entries = try? fileManager.contentsOfDirectory(at: directoryURL, includingPropertiesForKeys: nil, options: [.skipsHiddenFiles]) else {
   369→            return []
   370→        }
   371→
   372→        var consumers: [DiskSpaceConsumer] = []
   373→        for entry in entries {
   374→            let size = sizeOfItem(at: entry)
   375→            guard size > 0 else { continue }
   376→            consumers.append(DiskSpaceConsumer(
   377→                name: entry.lastPathComponent,
   378→                path: entry.path,
   379→                size: size
   380→            ))
   381→        }
   382→
   383→        return Array(consumers.sorted { $0.size > $1.size }.prefix(maxCount))
   384→    }
   385→
   386→    private func sizeOfItem(at url: URL) -> UInt64 {
   387→        let fileManager = FileManager.default
   388→        var isDirectory: ObjCBool = false
   389→        guard fileManager.fileExists(atPath: url.path, isDirectory: &isDirectory) else { return 0 }
   390→
   391→        let keys: Set<URLResourceKey> = [
   392→            .isRegularFileKey,
   393→            .isSymbolicLinkKey,
   394→            .totalFileAllocatedSizeKey,
   395→            .fileAllocatedSizeKey,
   396→            .totalFileSizeKey,
   397→            .fileSizeKey
   398→        ]
   399→
   400→        if !isDirectory.boolValue {
   401→            return fileSize(for: url, keys: keys)
   402→        }
   403→
   404→        guard let enumerator = fileManager.enumerator(
   405→            at: url,
   406→            includingPropertiesForKeys: Array(keys),
   407→            options: [.skipsPackageDescendants],
   408→            errorHandler: { _, _ in true }
   409→        ) else {
   410→            return 0
   411→        }
   412→
   413→        var total: UInt64 = 0
   414→        for case let fileURL as URL in enumerator {
   415→            autoreleasepool {
   416→                total += fileSize(for: fileURL, keys: keys)
   417→            }
   418→        }
   419→        return total
   420→    }
   421→
   422→    private func fileSize(for url: URL, keys: Set<URLResourceKey>) -> UInt64 {
   423→        guard let values = try? url.resourceValues(forKeys: keys) else { return 0 }
   424→        guard values.isSymbolicLink != true, values.isRegularFile == true else { return 0 }
   425→
   426→        let size = values.totalFileAllocatedSize
   427→            ?? values.fileAllocatedSize
   428→            ?? values.totalFileSize
   429→            ?? values.fileSize
   430→            ?? 0
   431→
   432→        return UInt64(max(size, 0))
   433→    }
   434→
   435→    private func formatBytes(_ bytes: UInt64) -> String {
   436→        let formatter = ByteCountFormatter()
   437→        formatter.countStyle = .file
   438→        return formatter.string(fromByteCount: Int64(bytes))
   439→    }
   440→
   441→    private func readDiskIOCounters() -> (read: UInt64, write: UInt64)? {
   442→        guard let matching = IOServiceMatching("IOBlockStorageDriver") else { return nil }
   443→
   444→        var iterator: io_iterator_t = 0
   445→        let result = IOServiceGetMatchingServices(kIOMainPortDefault, matching, &iterator)
   446→        guard result == KERN_SUCCESS else { return nil }
   447→        defer { IOObjectRelease(iterator) }
   448→
   449→        var totalRead: UInt64 = 0
   450→        var totalWrite: UInt64 = 0
   451→
   452→        var service = IOIteratorNext(iterator)
   453→        while service != 0 {
   454→            if let statsRef = IORegistryEntryCreateCFProperty(service, "Statistics" as CFString, kCFAllocatorDefault, 0),
   455→               let stats = statsRef.takeRetainedValue() as? [String: Any] {
   456→                totalRead += uint64Value(from: stats["Bytes (Read)"])
   457→                totalWrite += uint64Value(from: stats["Bytes (Write)"])
   458→            }
   459→
   460→            IOObjectRelease(service)
   461→            service = IOIteratorNext(iterator)
   462→        }
   463→
   464→        return (totalRead, totalWrite)
   465→    }
   466→
   467→    private func uint64Value(from value: Any?) -> UInt64 {
   468→        switch value {
   469→        case let number as NSNumber:
   470→            return number.uint64Value
   471→        case let value as UInt64:
   472→            return value
   473→        case let value as Int:
   474→            return UInt64(max(0, value))
   475→        default:
   476→            return 0
   477→        }
   478→    }
   479→}
   480→

1→import Foundation
     2→
     3→@Observable
     4→class ProcessMonitor {
     5→    var current: ProcessMetrics?
     6→    var history: [ProcessMetrics] = []
     7→
     8→    private var timer: Timer?
     9→    private var previousCPUTimesByPID: [pid_t: UInt64] = [:]
    10→    private var previousSampleTimestamp: Date?
    11→
    12→    func start(interval: TimeInterval = 3.0) {
    13→        timer?.invalidate()
    14→        timer = Timer.scheduledTimer(withTimeInterval: interval, repeats: true) { [weak self] _ in
    15→            self?.update()
    16→        }
    17→        update()
    18→    }
    19→
    20→    func stop() {
    21→        timer?.invalidate()
    22→        timer = nil
    23→    }
    24→
    25→    private func update() {
    26→        guard let metrics = fetchMetrics() else { return }
    27→
    28→        current = metrics
    29→        history.append(metrics)
    30→
    31→        if history.count > 120 {
    32→            history.removeFirst()
    33→        }
    34→    }
    35→
    36→    private func fetchMetrics() -> ProcessMetrics? {
    37→        let sampleTime = Date()
    38→        let elapsed = previousSampleTimestamp.map { sampleTime.timeIntervalSince($0) } ?? 0
    39→        let maxCPUPercent = Double(ProcessInfo.processInfo.activeProcessorCount) * 100.0
    40→        var currentCPUTimesByPID: [pid_t: UInt64] = [:]
    41→
    42→        var pids = [pid_t](repeating: 0, count: 1024)
    43→        let count = proc_listallpids(&pids, Int32(pids.count * MemoryLayout<pid_t>.size))
    44→
    45→        guard count > 0 else { return nil }
    46→
    47→        let actualCount = Int(count) / MemoryLayout<pid_t>.size
    48→        let processes = pids.prefix(actualCount).compactMap { pid -> ProcessDetails? in
    49→            guard pid > 0 else { return nil }
    50→
    51→            var info = proc_taskinfo()
    52→            let size = MemoryLayout<proc_taskinfo>.size
    53→            let result = proc_pidinfo(pid, PROC_PIDTASKINFO, 0, &info, Int32(size))
    54→
    55→            guard result == Int32(size) else { return nil }
    56→
    57→            let name = processName(for: pid)
    58→
    59→            let memoryUsage = info.pti_resident_size
    60→            let totalCPUTime = UInt64(info.pti_total_user + info.pti_total_system)
    61→            currentCPUTimesByPID[pid] = totalCPUTime
    62→
    63→            // Only include processes with significant memory usage
    64→            guard memoryUsage > 1_000_000 else { return nil } // > 1MB
    65→
    66→            let cpuUsage: Double
    67→            if elapsed > 0, let previousCPUTime = previousCPUTimesByPID[pid], totalCPUTime >= previousCPUTime {
    68→                let deltaCPUTime = totalCPUTime - previousCPUTime
    69→                let deltaSeconds = Double(deltaCPUTime) / 1_000_000_000.0 // proc_taskinfo time is ns
    70→                cpuUsage = min(max((deltaSeconds / elapsed) * 100.0, 0), maxCPUPercent)
    71→            } else {
    72→                cpuUsage = 0
    73→            }
    74→
    75→            return ProcessDetails(
    76→                id: pid,
    77→                name: name,
    78→                executablePath: "",
    79→                cpuUsage: cpuUsage,
    80→                memoryUsage: memoryUsage,
    81→                state: .running
    82→            )
    83→        }
    84→
    85→        // Keep a useful working set for both memory and CPU-oriented views.
    86→        let topByMemory = processes.sorted { $0.memoryUsage > $1.memoryUsage }.prefix(60)
    87→        let topByCPU = processes.sorted { $0.cpuUsage > $1.cpuUsage }.prefix(60)
    88→
    89→        var seen = Set<pid_t>()
    90→        var topProcesses: [ProcessDetails] = []
    91→        for process in Array(topByCPU) + Array(topByMemory) {
    92→            if seen.insert(process.id).inserted {
    93→                topProcesses.append(process)
    94→            }
    95→            if topProcesses.count >= 100 {
    96→                break
    97→            }
    98→        }
    99→
   100→        previousCPUTimesByPID = currentCPUTimesByPID
   101→        previousSampleTimestamp = sampleTime
   102→
   103→        return ProcessMetrics(
   104→            timestamp: sampleTime,
   105→            processes: topProcesses
   106→        )
   107→    }
   108→
   109→    private func processName(for pid: pid_t) -> String {
   110→        var nameBuffer = [CChar](repeating: 0, count: Int(MAXPATHLEN))
   111→        let result = proc_name(pid, &nameBuffer, UInt32(nameBuffer.count))
   112→        guard result > 0 else { return "Unknown" }
   113→        return String(cString: nameBuffer)
   114→    }
   115→
   116→    static func executablePath(for pid: pid_t) -> String? {
   117→        var pathBuffer = [CChar](repeating: 0, count: Int(MAXPATHLEN))
   118→        let result = proc_pidpath(pid, &pathBuffer, UInt32(pathBuffer.count))
   119→        guard result > 0 else { return nil }
   120→        return String(cString: pathBuffer)
   121→    }
   122→}
   123→

1→import Foundation
     2→
     3→@Observable
     4→class MonitorCoordinator {
     5→    static let shared = MonitorCoordinator()
     6→
     7→    let cpu = CPUMonitor()
     8→    let memory = MemoryMonitor()
     9→    let network = NetworkMonitor()
    10→    let wifi = WiFiMonitor()
    11→    let disk = DiskMonitor()
    12→    let processes = ProcessMonitor()
    13→    let battery = BatteryMonitor()
    14→    private let settings = AppSettings.shared
    15→    private(set) var isPowerSaveMode = false
    16→
    17→    private struct PollingProfile {
    18→        let cpu: Double
    19→        let memory: Double
    20→        let network: Double
    21→        let wifi: Double
    22→        let disk: Double
    23→        let processes: Double
    24→        let battery: Double
    25→    }
    26→
    27→    var systemUptime: String {
    28→        let uptime = ProcessInfo.processInfo.systemUptime
    29→        let days = Int(uptime / 86400)
    30→        let hours = Int((uptime.truncatingRemainder(dividingBy: 86400)) / 3600)
    31→        let minutes = Int((uptime.truncatingRemainder(dividingBy: 3600)) / 60)
    32→        return "\(days)d \(hours)h \(minutes)m"
    33→    }
    34→
    35→    func startAll() {
    36→        applyPollingProfile()
    37→    }
    38→
    39→    func restartAll() {
    40→        stopAll()
    41→        applyPollingProfile()
    42→    }
    43→
    44→    func setPowerSaveMode(_ enabled: Bool) {
    45→        guard enabled != isPowerSaveMode else { return }
    46→        isPowerSaveMode = enabled
    47→        restartAll()
    48→    }
    49→
    50→    func stopAll() {
    51→        cpu.stop()
    52→        memory.stop()
    53→        network.stop()
    54→        wifi.stop()
    55→        disk.stop()
    56→        processes.stop()
    57→        battery.stop()
    58→    }
    59→
    60→    private func applyPollingProfile() {
    61→        let profile = currentPollingProfile()
    62→        cpu.start(interval: profile.cpu)
    63→        memory.start(interval: profile.memory)
    64→        network.start(interval: profile.network)
    65→        wifi.start(interval: profile.wifi)
    66→        disk.start(interval: profile.disk)
    67→        processes.start(interval: profile.processes)
    68→        battery.start(interval: profile.battery)
    69→    }
    70→
    71→    private func currentPollingProfile() -> PollingProfile {
    72→        guard isPowerSaveMode else {
    73→            return PollingProfile(
    74→                cpu: settings.cpuPollInterval,
    75→                memory: settings.memoryPollInterval,
    76→                network: settings.networkPollInterval,
    77→                wifi: settings.wifiPollInterval,
    78→                disk: settings.diskPollInterval,
    79→                processes: settings.processesPollInterval,
    80→                battery: settings.batteryPollInterval
    81→            )
    82→        }
    83→
    84→        // Low-power profile: reduce monitor update rates significantly.
    85→        return PollingProfile(
    86→            cpu: max(settings.cpuPollInterval * 3.0, 3.0),
    87→            memory: max(settings.memoryPollInterval * 2.5, 5.0),
    88→            network: max(settings.networkPollInterval * 4.0, 5.0),
    89→            wifi: max(settings.wifiPollInterval * 4.0, 5.0),
    90→            disk: max(settings.diskPollInterval * 2.0, 15.0),

    41→    func requestLocationPermission() {

    44→        DispatchQueue.main.async { [weak self] in

    46→            self.didAttemptLocationPermissionRequest = true

    48→            NSApp.activate(ignoringOtherApps: true)

    55→            DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + 1.5) { [weak self] in
    56→                guard let self else { return }

   141→    private func fetchMetrics() -> WiFiMetrics? {
   142→        let routerIP = getRouterIP()

   144→
   145→        guard let interface = client.interface() else {
   146→            print("WiFiMonitor: No WiFi interface available")
   147→            // Return empty metrics if no WiFi interface available
   148→            return WiFiMetrics(
   149→                timestamp: Date(),

   153→                linkRate: nil,

   164→                incident: nil
   165→            )
   166→        }
   167→
   168→        let ssid = interface.ssid()
   169→        let channel = interface.wlanChannel()
   170→        print("WiFiMonitor: interface=\(interface.interfaceName ?? "unknown"), ssid=\(ssid ?? "nil"), channel=\(channel?.channelNumber ?? 0), band=\(channel?.channelBand.rawValue ?? 0)")
   171→
   172→        let band: WiFiBand?
   173→        if let channel = interface.wlanChannel() {
   174→            switch channel.channelBand {
   175→            case .band2GHz: band = .band2_4GHz
   176→            case .band5GHz: band = .band5GHz
   177→            case .band6GHz: band = .band6GHz
   178→            default: band = nil
   179→            }
   180→        } else {
   181→            band = nil
   182→        }

   184→        let metrics = WiFiMetrics(
   185→            timestamp: Date(),
   186→            ssid: ssid,
   187→            routerIP: routerIP,
   188→            band: band,
   189→            linkRate: Double(interface.transmitRate()),

   191→            noiseFloor: interface.noiseMeasurement(),

   195→            internetPing: internetPingEngine?.currentPing,
   196→            internetJitter: internetPingEngine?.jitter,
   197→            internetPacketLoss: internetPingEngine?.packetLoss,
   198→            dnsLookupTime: measureDNSLookup(),

   236→    private func getRouterIP() -> String? {
   237→        guard let store = dynamicStore else { return nil }
   238→
   239→        if let primaryService = primaryServiceID(from: store),
   240→           let ipv4 = SCDynamicStoreCopyValue(store, "State:/Network/Service/\(primaryService)/IPv4" as CFString) as? [String: Any],
   241→           let router = ipv4["Router"] as? String,
   242→           !router.isEmpty {
   243→            return router
   244→        }
   245→
   246→        if let globalIPv4 = SCDynamicStoreCopyValue(store, "State:/Network/Global/IPv4" as CFString) as? [String: Any],
   247→           let router = globalIPv4["Router"] as? String,
   248→           !router.isEmpty {
   249→            return router
   250→        }
   251→
   252→        return nil
   253→    }
   254→
   255→    private func primaryServiceID(from store: SCDynamicStore) -> String? {
   256→        guard let globalIPv4 = SCDynamicStoreCopyValue(store, "State:/Network/Global/IPv4" as CFString) as? [String: Any] else {
   257→            return nil
   258→        }
   259→        return globalIPv4["PrimaryService"] as? String
   260→    }
   261→
   262→
   263→    private func measureDNSLookup() -> Double? {
   264→        let start = Date()
   265→        var result: UnsafeMutablePointer<addrinfo>?
   266→        let status = getaddrinfo("google.com", nil, nil, &result)
   267→        if let result {
   268→            freeaddrinfo(result)
   269→        }
   270→        guard status == 0 else { return nil }
   271→        return Date().timeIntervalSince(start) * 1000
   272→    }
   273→
   274→    private func getDNSServer() -> String? {
   275→        guard let store = dynamicStore else { return nil }
   276→
   277→        if let primaryService = primaryServiceID(from: store),
   278→           let dns = SCDynamicStoreCopyValue(store, "State:/Network/Service/\(primaryService)/DNS" as CFString) as? [String: Any],
   279→           let servers = dns["ServerAddresses"] as? [String],
   280→           let first = servers.first,
   281→           !first.isEmpty {
   282→            return first
   283→        }
   284→
   285→        if let globalDNS = SCDynamicStoreCopyValue(store, "State:/Network/Global/DNS" as CFString) as? [String: Any],
   286→           let servers = globalDNS["ServerAddresses"] as? [String],
   287→           let first = servers.first,
   288→           !first.isEmpty {
   289→            return first
   290→        }
   291→
   292→        return nil
   293→    }

   295→    private func openLocationPrivacySettings() {
   296→        let urls = [
   297→            "x-apple.systempreferences:com.apple.preference.security?Privacy_LocationServices",
   298→            "x-apple.systempreferences:com.apple.settings.PrivacySecurity.extension?Privacy_LocationServices"
   299→        ]
   300→
   301→        for rawURL in urls {
   302→            guard let url = URL(string: rawURL) else { continue }
   303→            if NSWorkspace.shared.open(url) {
   304→                return
   305→            }
   306→        }
   307→    }
   308→}

1→import Foundation
     2→import IOKit.ps
     3→import IOKit
     4→
     5→@Observable
     6→class BatteryMonitor {
     7→    var current: BatteryMetrics?
     8→    var history: [BatteryMetrics] = []
     9→
    10→    private var timer: Timer?
    11→    private var smcReader: SMCReader?
    12→
    13→    func start(interval: TimeInterval = 10.0) {
    14→        smcReader = SMCReader()
    15→        timer?.invalidate()
    16→        timer = Timer.scheduledTimer(withTimeInterval: interval, repeats: true) { [weak self] _ in
    17→            self?.update()
    18→        }
    19→        update()
    20→    }
    21→
    22→    func stop() {
    23→        timer?.invalidate()
    24→        timer = nil
    25→    }
    26→
    27→    private func update() {
    28→        guard let metrics = fetchMetrics() else { return }
    29→
    30→        current = metrics
    31→        history.append(metrics)
    32→
    33→        if history.count > 120 {
    34→            history.removeFirst()
    35→        }
    36→    }
    37→
    38→    private func fetchMetrics() -> BatteryMetrics? {
    39→        guard let snapshot = IOPSCopyPowerSourcesInfo()?.takeRetainedValue(),
    40→              let sources = IOPSCopyPowerSourcesList(snapshot)?.takeRetainedValue() as? [CFTypeRef],
    41→              !sources.isEmpty else {
    42→            return nil
    43→        }
    44→
    45→        var batteryInfo: [String: Any]?
    46→
    47→        for source in sources {
    48→            if let info = IOPSGetPowerSourceDescription(snapshot, source)?.takeUnretainedValue() as? [String: Any],
    49→               let type = info[kIOPSTypeKey] as? String,
    50→               type == kIOPSInternalBatteryType {
    51→                batteryInfo = info
    52→                break
    53→            }
    54→        }
    55→
    56→        guard let info = batteryInfo else { return nil }
    57→
    58→        let currentCapacity = info[kIOPSCurrentCapacityKey] as? Int ?? 0
    59→        let _ = info[kIOPSMaxCapacityKey] as? Int ?? 100
    60→        let isCharging = (info[kIOPSIsChargingKey] as? Bool) ?? false
    61→        let timeToEmpty = info[kIOPSTimeToEmptyKey] as? Int ?? -1
    62→        let timeToFull = info[kIOPSTimeToFullChargeKey] as? Int ?? -1
    63→        let powerSource = (info[kIOPSPowerSourceStateKey] as? String) == kIOPSACPowerValue ? PowerSource.ac : PowerSource.battery
    64→
    65→        // Try to get cycle count and health from IOKit
    66→        var cycleCount = 0
    67→        var health = 100.0
    68→
    69→        let service = IOServiceGetMatchingService(kIOMainPortDefault, IOServiceMatching("AppleSmartBattery"))
    70→        if service != 0 {
    71→            if let cycleCountRef = IORegistryEntryCreateCFProperty(service, "CycleCount" as CFString, kCFAllocatorDefault, 0) {
    72→                cycleCount = (cycleCountRef.takeRetainedValue() as? Int) ?? 0
    73→            }
    74→
    75→            // MaxCapacity from IORegistry is already a percentage (0-100)!
    76→            if let maxCapacityRef = IORegistryEntryCreateCFProperty(service, "MaxCapacity" as CFString, kCFAllocatorDefault, 0) {
    77→                health = Double((maxCapacityRef.takeRetainedValue() as? Int) ?? 100)
    78→            }
    79→
    80→            IOObjectRelease(service)
    81→        }
    82→
    83→        let chargePercent = Double(currentCapacity)
    84→
    85→        let timeEstimateMinutes = isCharging ? timeToFull : timeToEmpty
    86→
    87→        return BatteryMetrics(
    88→            timestamp: Date(),
    89→            chargePercent: chargePercent,
    90→            isCharging: isCharging,
    91→            health: health,
    92→            cycleCount: cycleCount,
    93→            temperature: smcReader?.getBatteryTemperature(),
    94→            timeRemaining: timeEstimateMinutes > 0 ? TimeInterval(timeEstimateMinutes * 60) : nil,
    95→            powerDraw: nil,
    96→            powerSource: powerSource
    97→        )
    98→    }
    99→}

     3→class SparklineRenderer {
     4→    static func render(data: [Double], size: NSSize, color: NSColor = .systemGreen) -> NSImage? {
     5→        guard !data.isEmpty else { return nil }
     6→
     7→        let image = NSImage(size: size)
     8→        image.lockFocus()
     9→
    10→        let context = NSGraphicsContext.current?.cgContext
    11→        context?.clear(CGRect(origin: .zero, size: size))
    12→
    13→        // Find min/max for scaling
    14→        let maxValue = data.max() ?? 100
    15→        let minValue = data.min() ?? 0
    16→        let range = max(maxValue - minValue, 1)
    17→
    18→        // Create path
    19→        let path = NSBezierPath()
    20→        let xStep = size.width / CGFloat(max(data.count - 1, 1))
    21→
    22→        for (index, value) in data.enumerated() {
    23→            let x = CGFloat(index) * xStep
    24→            let normalizedValue = (value - minValue) / range
    25→            let y = CGFloat(normalizedValue) * size.height
    26→
    27→            if index == 0 {
    28→                path.move(to: NSPoint(x: x, y: y))
    29→            } else {
    30→                path.line(to: NSPoint(x: x, y: y))
    31→            }
    32→        }
    33→
    34→        // Draw gradient fill
    35→        let gradientColors = [
    36→            color.withAlphaComponent(0.6),
    37→            color.withAlphaComponent(0.0)
    38→        ]
    39→        let gradient = NSGradient(colors: gradientColors)
    40→
    41→        context?.saveGState()
    42→
    43→        // Create fill path
    44→        let fillPath = path.copy() as! NSBezierPath
    45→        fillPath.line(to: NSPoint(x: size.width, y: 0))
    46→        fillPath.line(to: NSPoint(x: 0, y: 0))
    47→        fillPath.close()
    48→
    49→        fillPath.addClip()
    50→        gradient?.draw(from: NSPoint(x: 0, y: size.height), to: NSPoint(x: 0, y: 0), options: [])
    51→
    52→        context?.restoreGState()
    53→
    54→        // Draw line
    55→        color.setStroke()
    56→        path.lineWidth = 1.5
    57→        path.stroke()
    58→
    59→        image.unlockFocus()
    60→        return image
    61→    }

content.text:
### Initialize Sentry SDK in Objective-C

Source: https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-cocoa/blob/main/README.md

Initializes the Sentry SDK using Objective-C with a provided DSN and enables debug logs. This initialization should occur early in the application's lifecycle, typically within the AppDelegate.

```objective-c
@import Sentry;

// ....

[SentrySDK startWithConfigureOptions:^(SentryOptions *options) {
    options.dsn = @"___PUBLIC_DSN___";
    options.debug = @YES; // Helpful to see what's going on
}];
```

--------------------------------

### Runtime Configuration Overrides in Swift

Source: https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-cocoa/blob/main/Samples/README.md

Demonstrates how to modify Sentry SDK configuration at runtime using UserDefaults in Swift. These overrides interact with schema launch arguments and environment variables, with UserDefaults values taking precedence by default. Boolean flags are ORed, and all environment variables are written as strings.

```swift
SentrySDKWrapper.swift
SentrySDKOverrides.swift
```

--------------------------------

### Core Sentry Components and Functionality

Source: https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-cocoa/blob/main/develop-docs/ARCHITECTURE.md

This snippet highlights the fundamental classes and wrappers that form the Sentry Cocoa SDK's core architecture. These components manage configuration, metadata, and the overall communication with the Sentry backend.

```swift
SentrySDK
SentryOptions
SentryScope
SentryHub
SentryClient
```

--------------------------------

### Swift Modulemap Configuration

Source: https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-cocoa/blob/main/develop-docs/SWIFT.md

Shows the configuration for a private modulemap file (_SentryPrivate) used to expose internal Objective-C headers to Swift. This involves pointing to a header file that includes all private Objective-C headers.

```text
module _SentryPrivate {
    header "SentryPrivate.h"
    export *
    use module ObjectiveC
}
```

--------------------------------

### Error Handling: Missing Private Module

Source: https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-cocoa/blob/main/develop-docs/SWIFT.md

This code snippet demonstrates a common error that occurs when the _SentryPrivate module is not correctly linked or imported in Swift, leading to a 'no such module' error.

```swift
Sentry.swiftmodule/arm64-apple-ios.private.swiftinterface:10:8: error: no such module '_SentryPrivate'

import _SentryPrivate
```

input.query: install sentry-cocoa Swift Package Manager SPM package URL macOS specific considerations

content.text:
### Add Sentry SDK via Swift Package Manager (SPM) in Xcode

Source: https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-docs/blob/master/docs/platforms/apple/common/install/swift-package-manager.mdx

This snippet shows how to add the Sentry SDK to your Xcode project using Swift Package Manager. You can add the SDK by entering the Git repository URL in Xcode's package dependency manager. This method allows for easy integration and version management of the Sentry SDK.

```text
https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-cocoa.git
```

--------------------------------

### Build Sentry SDK Without UI Frameworks using Package.swift

Source: https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-docs/blob/master/docs/platforms/apple/common/install/swift-package-manager.mdx

This Swift code snippet shows how to configure the Sentry SDK dependency in `Package.swift` to exclude UI frameworks, using the `NoUIFramework` trait. This is essential for building applications that do not rely on UIKit, AppKit, or SwiftUI, such as command-line tools or server-side applications. It also specifies the `SentrySPM` product for the executable target.

```swift
let package = Package(
    name: "MyApp",
    dependencies: [
        .package(
            url: "https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-cocoa",
            from: "{{@inject packages.version('sentry.cocoa') }}",
            traits: ["NoUIFramework"] 
        ),
    ],
    targets: [
        .executableTarget(
            name: "MyApp",
            dependencies: [
                .product(name: "SentrySPM", package: "sentry-cocoa"),
            ]
        ),
    ]
)
```

--------------------------------

### Capture Uncaught NSExceptions on macOS with Sentry Apple SDK

Source: https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-docs/blob/master/includes/apple-platform-getting-started.mdx

This snippet demonstrates how to configure the Sentry Apple SDK to capture uncaught NSExceptions on macOS. It involves setting the `enableUncaughtNSExceptionReporting` option to true. This is crucial as the SDK cannot capture these exceptions by default.

```swift
import Sentry

SentrySDK.start { options in
    options.dsn = "___PUBLIC_DSN___"
    options.enableUncaughtNSExceptionReporting = true
}
```

```objc
@import Sentry;

[SentrySDK startWithConfigureOptions:^(SentryOptions *options) {
    options.dsn = @"___PUBLIC_DSN___";
    options.enableUncaughtNSExceptionReporting = YES;
}];
```

### Swift Package Manager (SPM)

Source: https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-docs/blob/master/docs/platforms/apple/common/install/swift-package-manager.mdx

To integrate Sentry into your Xcode project using Swift Package Manager (SPM), open your App in Xcode and open **File > Add Packages**. Then add the SDK by entering the git repo url in the top right search field: `https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-cocoa.git`. You can define your dependency rule by selecting the SDK version (or branch), and then click the "Add Package" button. You will then be prompted to choose one of the options `Sentry`, `Sentry-Dynamic` or `SentrySwiftUI`. `Sentry` is the static framework, which is the recommended option if you prefer a fast app start time. `Sentry-Dynamic` is the dynamic framework. `SentrySwiftUI` is used to track performance of SwiftUI views.

--------------------------------

### Apple SDK > Install > General

Source: https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-docs/blob/master/includes/apple-platform-getting-started.mdx

For platforms where the Sentry Wizard is not the primary recommendation, you can integrate the SDK using Swift Package Manager (SPM). By navigating to File > Add Packages in Xcode and providing the Sentry Cocoa repository URL, you can incorporate the SDK into your project. While SPM is the recommended method for these platforms, alternative installation methods are supported to accommodate different project structures and requirements.

Here is the complete research report.

---

## 1. SPM Package URL for sentry-cocoa

```
https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-cocoa.git
```

When adding via Xcode: **File > Add Packages**, paste the URL above. You will be prompted to choose one of three products:
- **Sentry** -- static framework (recommended for fast app start time)
- **Sentry-Dynamic** -- dynamic framework
- **SentrySwiftUI** -- for SwiftUI view performance tracking

For your menu bar app, **Sentry** (static) is the best choice.

## 2. Current Project State

The project at `/Users/modestnerd/Developer/Projects/WhatTheLoad` does **not** currently use SPM. There is:
- No `Package.swift` file
- No `Package.resolved` file
- No `XCRemoteSwiftPackageReference` or `XCSwiftPackageProductDependency` entries in the `.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj`

The project is a pure Xcode project (`WhatTheLoad.xcodeproj`) with zero external package dependencies.

## 3. App Lifecycle

The app uses a hybrid approach at `/Users/modestnerd/Developer/Projects/WhatTheLoad/WhatTheLoad/WhatTheLoadApp.swift`:
- `@main struct WhatTheLoadApp: App` with `@NSApplicationDelegateAdaptor(AppDelegate.self)`
- The real setup happens in `AppDelegate.applicationDidFinishLaunching(_:)` at `/Users/modestnerd/Developer/Projects/WhatTheLoad/WhatTheLoad/App/AppDelegate.swift`

## 4. Minimal Setup Code for Your App

Since your `AppDelegate` uses `NSApplicationDelegate` (not `UIApplicationDelegate`), the integration point is `applicationDidFinishLaunching`. Add this at the very top of that method:

```swift
import Sentry

// In AppDelegate.swift, at the start of applicationDidFinishLaunching:
func applicationDidFinishLaunching(_ notification: Notification) {
    SentrySDK.start { options in
        options.dsn = "https://your-key@your-org.ingest.sentry.io/your-project-id"
        options.debug = true // Remove or set to false in production
        options.tracesSampleRate = 1.0 // Adjust in production (e.g., 0.2)
        options.enableUncaughtNSExceptionReporting = true // macOS-specific, see below
    }

    // ... rest of your existing setup
    NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.accessory)
    // ...
}
```

## 5. macOS-Specific Considerations

**Critical -- Uncaught NSException Reporting:** On macOS, the Sentry SDK **cannot** capture uncaught `NSException`s by default. You must explicitly enable this:

```swift
options.enableUncaughtNSExceptionReporting = true
```

This is the single most important macOS-specific setting. Without it, many AppKit-originating crashes will be silently lost.

**Other considerations for your app specifically:**

- **Menu bar / accessory app:** Your app runs as `.accessory` (no dock icon). Sentry works fine in this mode -- it does not require a visible app window.
- **Session Replay:** The `sessionReplay` options reference UIKit and are iOS-only. Do not configure `options.sessionReplay` on macOS.
- **NoUIFramework trait:** If you were using a `Package.swift` directly (e.g., for a CLI tool), you could pass `traits: ["NoUIFramework"]` to exclude UIKit/AppKit dependencies. Since you are using Xcode's SPM integration for a GUI app, this does not apply to you.
- **SPM product choice:** Use `Sentry` (static), not `SentrySwiftUI`, since your menu bar popover views are not doing SwiftUI performance tracing via Sentry.

- **Menu bar / accessory app:** Your app runs as `.accessory` (no dock icon). Sentry works fine in this mode -- it does not require a visible app window.
- **Session Replay:** The `sessionReplay` options reference UIKit and are iOS-only. Do not configure `options.sessionReplay` on macOS.
- **NoUIFramework trait:** If you were using a `Package.swift` directly (e.g., for a CLI tool), you could pass `traits: ["NoUIFramework"]` to exclude UIKit/AppKit dependencies. Since you are using Xcode's SPM integration for a GUI app, this does not apply to you.
- **SPM product choice:** Use `Sentry` (static), not `SentrySwiftUI`, since your menu bar popover views are not doing SwiftUI performance tracing via Sentry.

---

## Performance Analysis: WhatTheLoad

### Current State (Baseline)

The app runs **9 concurrent timers** in normal mode at the following rates:
- CPU monitor: 1s
- Network monitor: 1s
- WiFi monitor: 1s
- Memory monitor: 2s
- Process monitor: 3s
- Disk monitor: 5s
- Battery monitor: 10s
- MenuBar controller: 1s (separate from monitors)
- AlertRulesEngine: 15s
- HistoryStore snapshots: 60s
- AppDelegate: 2 extra timers at 15s and 20s (low battery + battery automation)

That is **11 active timers** in total. Here is a breakdown of every bottleneck found, ranked by severity.

---

## Critical Bottlenecks

### 1. WiFiMonitor: Blocking DNS lookup on the main thread every second

**File:** `WiFiMonitor.swift`, line 198 / `fetchMetrics()`, line 263-271

```swift
dnsLookupTime: measureDNSLookup(),
```

```swift
private func measureDNSLookup() -> Double? {
    let start = Date()
    var result: UnsafeMutablePointer<addrinfo>?
    let status = getaddrinfo("google.com", nil, nil, &result)
    ...
}
```

`getaddrinfo` is a **synchronous, blocking network call** — it can take anywhere from 1ms to several hundred milliseconds. It is called directly inside `fetchMetrics()`, which is invoked from the WiFi timer. WiFi polls at **1 second**. Since `Timer.scheduledTimer` fires on the **main run loop**, this is blocking the main thread up to once per second.

**Fix:** Move DNS measurement to a background queue, cache the result with a 5-10s TTL, and populate it asynchronously into the metrics struct.

---

### 2. NetworkMonitor: Spawning two shell processes every 5 seconds on the main thread

**File:** `NetworkMonitor.swift`, lines 162-188 (`fetchActiveConnectionsViaNetstat`)

```swift
let tcpOutput = runCommand("/usr/sbin/netstat", arguments: ["-an", "-p", "tcp"]) ?? ""
let udpOutput = runCommand("/usr/sbin/netstat", arguments: ["-an", "-p", "udp"]) ?? ""
```

`runCommand` at line 191 uses `Process()` + `pipe.fileHandleForReading.readDataToEndOfFile()` + `process.waitUntilExit()` — all **synchronous blocking calls**. Two `netstat` processes are spawned and awaited. `netstat -an` on a machine with many connections can produce thousands of lines and take 50-200ms per call. This happens every 5s (cached), but runs on the main thread because `NetworkMonitor.update()` is called from a main-run-loop `Timer`.

**Fix:** Run `fetchActiveConnectionsViaNetstat` on a background `DispatchQueue`, update `cachedActiveConnections` with the result via `DispatchQueue.main.async`, and return the cached value immediately in `getActiveConnections()`. The existing 5s cache TTL is already there — just make the refresh async.

---

### 3. HistoryStore: Full JSON encode + atomic write of up to 20,000 snapshots on every event

**File:** `HistoryStore.swift`, lines 33-37 and 117-145

```swift
func recordEvent(_ event: TimelineEvent) {
    events.insert(event, at: 0)
    pruneToRetentionWindow()
    persistToDisk()  // <-- triggered immediately on every single event
}
```

```swift
if let encodedSnapshots = try? encoder.encode(snapshotsCopy) {
    try? encodedSnapshots.write(to: snapshotsURL, options: .atomic)
}
```

This is already dispatched to `ioQueue` (good), but there are two issues:

1. **`encoder.outputFormatting = [.prettyPrinted]`** (line 137): Pretty-printing 20,000 JSON objects is significantly slower than compact encoding. With 20k snapshots at max capacity, pretty-printed JSON can easily be 5-10x larger and slower to encode than compact JSON. There is no human reader for these files — they are machine-read only.

2. **Write on every event**: `AlertRulesEngine.evaluateRules()` calls `historyStore.recordEvent()` at a 15s cadence, and `evaluateIncidentState()` can trigger events during normal polling. Each call encodes and atomically writes the **entire** snapshot array (potentially megabytes). The disk I/O and memory allocation cost accumulates.

**Fix 1:** Remove `.prettyPrinted` from the encoder — use compact output.

**Fix 2:** Debounce `persistToDisk()` with a short delay (e.g., 2-3 seconds). Multiple rapid events (alert trigger + incident state change) will coalesce into a single write. The snapshots-only path already uses a 60s timer, so you mainly need debouncing on event-triggered writes.

---

### 4. MenuBarController: Reallocates NSImage symbols every second

**File:** `MenuBarController.swift`, lines 72-82 and 192-211

Every second, for each active menu bar item, `appendIcon()` calls:
```swift
NSImage(systemSymbolName: symbolName, accessibilityDescription: nil)?
    .withSymbolConfiguration(config)
```

And `batteryImage(for:)` calls the same pattern, creating a fresh `NSImage` + `NSImage.SymbolConfiguration` object every tick. The `NSAttributedString` + `NSTextAttachment` objects are also recreated from scratch each second.

The symbol image only changes when the battery level crosses a threshold (~25/50/75%). CPU/memory/disk icons never change at all. This is pure unnecessary allocation on every 1s tick.

**Fix:** Cache the symbol images keyed by their symbol name. Keep a `private var cachedSymbols: [String: NSImage] = [:]` dictionary. Only rebuild when the symbol name would change (battery crossing a band). For static icons (CPU, memory, disk), create them once in `init` or lazily on first access.

---

### 5. CPUMonitor: Hardcoded stub for `fetchFrequency()`

**File:** `CPUMonitor.swift`, lines 167-170

```swift
private func fetchFrequency() -> Double? {
    // Would use sysctl to get current CPU frequency
    return 3.2
}
```

This is a constant stub that returns `3.2` on every call. It's harmless overhead-wise, but the comment "Would use sysctl..." means it was intended to be implemented. More importantly, it creates a `CPUMetrics` struct embedding this fake value every second and storing 120 entries in `history`. If frequency is never real, the field should be `nil` or removed to avoid misleading data in the history store and disk snapshots.

---

## High Impact Issues

### 6. ProcessMonitor: Allocates a fixed 1024-element PID buffer every 3 seconds

**File:** `ProcessMonitor.swift`, line 42

```swift
var pids = [pid_t](repeating: 0, count: 1024)
```

This creates a new `[Int32]` array of 1024 elements (4KB) on every poll. On a system with many processes this may silently truncate (the count at line 43 is divided by `MemoryLayout<pid_t>.size` which is 4, so it's returning `count/4` = max 256 PIDs from a 1024-element buffer). The actual `proc_listallpids` call takes the byte size of the buffer, so a 1024-element buffer only holds 1024/4 = 256 PIDs. A loaded Mac can easily have 400+ processes.

**Fix:** Either make this a persistent property that avoids reallocation, or use the two-call pattern: call `proc_listallpids(nil, 0)` first to get the count, then allocate exactly that size.

---

### 7. ProcessMonitor: Two full sorts of potentially hundreds of `ProcessDetails` every 3 seconds

**File:** `ProcessMonitor.swift`, lines 86-87

```swift
let topByMemory = processes.sorted { $0.memoryUsage > $1.memoryUsage }.prefix(60)
let topByCPU = processes.sorted { $0.cpuUsage > $1.cpuUsage }.prefix(60)
```

Two O(n log n) sorts of the full process list, then a deduplication loop, to produce the final 100. With 200+ processes this runs every 3s.

**Fix:** Use `partialSort` — since you only need the top 60, `Array.sort` is over-engineered. Use `processes.min(count: 60, by:)` via `Sequence` or implement a partial heap select. Alternatively, a single sort by a combined score would let you produce both views in one pass.

---

### 8. WiFiMonitor: `getRouterIP()` called twice per update cycle

**File:** `WiFiMonitor.swift`, lines 119 and 142

```swift
private func update() {
    refreshRouterPingEngineIfNeeded()   // calls getRouterIP() at line 228
    guard let metrics = fetchMetrics()  // fetchMetrics() calls getRouterIP() at line 142
```

`getRouterIP()` does two `SCDynamicStoreCopyValue` calls to the system network configuration store. This runs twice per 1s tick — once in `refreshRouterPingEngineIfNeeded()` and once in `fetchMetrics()`.

**Fix:** Call `getRouterIP()` once at the start of `update()`, store in a local variable, and pass it into both callsites.

---

### 9. WiFiMonitor: Debug `print` statements in the hot path (every second)

**File:** `WiFiMonitor.swift`, lines 88, 170, 319, 326

```swift
print("WiFiMonitor: interface=\(interface.interfaceName ?? "unknown"), ssid=\(ssid ?? "nil"), ...")
```

`print` to stdout on a background timer is benign in isolation, but there are 4 print calls in WiFiMonitor alone, including one inside `fetchMetrics()` which runs every second. String interpolation with optional chaining creates transient string objects on every call, even when no one is watching stdout. This is a menu bar app — stdout is discarded in production.

**Fix:** Remove all production `print` statements or wrap them in `#if DEBUG`.

---

### 10. MemoryMonitor: `sysctlbyname("hw.memsize")` called every 2 seconds

**File:** `MemoryMonitor.swift`, lines 55-57

```swift
var size: UInt64 = 0
var sizeLength = MemoryLayout<UInt64>.size
sysctlbyname("hw.memsize", &size, &sizeLength, nil, 0)
```

`hw.memsize` is the total physical RAM. It never changes. This syscall is made on every 2s poll.

**Fix:** Cache this value in a `private let totalMemory: UInt64` fetched once in `init()` or the first `update()` call.

---

### 11. DiskMonitor: Full filesystem enumeration on startup (and after every cleanup)

**File:** `DiskMonitor.swift`, lines 38-40 and 123

```swift
func start(...) {
    ...
    refreshSpaceAnalysis()  // <-- runs immediately on startup
}
```

`buildSpaceAnalysis` enumerates `~/Library/Caches`, `~/Library/Application Support`, `~/Library/Containers`, `~/Downloads`, `~/Documents`, `~/Desktop`, `~/Movies`, `~/Pictures`, `~/Developer`, `~/.Trash` using recursive `FileManager.enumerator`. On a large developer machine with 50GB+ of DerivedData, this is an extremely expensive operation. It's correctly dispatched to a background queue, but it runs immediately at app launch and again after every cache-cleanup action (`refreshSpaceAnalysis()` at lines 123 and 208).

This is already managed with `isAnalyzingSpace` guard, but there is no cooldown after app launch. If the user opens the app quickly, a large enumeration is queued immediately.

**Fix:** Add a minimum 30-second delay before the first `refreshSpaceAnalysis()` on `start()`, and enforce a minimum re-analysis interval (currently `lastSpaceAnalysisAt` is tracked but never checked before re-triggering after cleanup).

---

### 12. AppSettings.menuBarItems: String parsed on every access

**File:** `AppSettings.swift`, lines 48-55

```swift
var menuBarItems: [MenuBarItem] {
    get {
        menuBarItemsRaw.split(separator: ",").compactMap { MenuBarItem(rawValue: String($0)) }
    }
```

`MenuBarController.updateStatusItem()` reads `settings.menuBarItems` (line 30) on every 1s tick. Each access splits a string and maps through an initializer to create a new `[MenuBarItem]` array. This is cheap individually, but is pure waste — the items don't change during normal operation.

**Fix:** Cache the parsed result. Observe changes to `menuBarItemsRaw` (via `@AppStorage` publisher or `didSet`) and invalidate the cache only when the raw string changes.

---

### 13. HistoryStore: `Array.removeFirst()` on large arrays

**File:** `CPUMonitor.swift` line 43, `MemoryMonitor.swift` line 29, `NetworkMonitor.swift` line 44, all monitors

```swift
if history.count > 120 {
    history.removeFirst()
}
```

Every monitor uses `Array.removeFirst()` which is O(n) — it shifts all 119 remaining elements. Called every 1s for CPU/Network, every 2s for Memory. While 120 elements is small enough that this is not a crisis, a circular buffer (`RingBuffer<T>` or a simple index-wrapped array) would be more correct. Alternatively, `history.removeFirst()` on a 120-element array at 1s cadence means ~7200 element-shift operations per minute across all monitors.

**Fix:** Replace the history arrays with a `[T]` backed by a fixed-capacity circular write index, or use `history.dropFirst()` with `ArraySlice` and only reallocate periodically.

---

## Summary Table

| # | Issue | File | Severity | Impact |
|---|-------|------|----------|--------|
| 1 | Blocking DNS lookup on main thread every 1s | `WiFiMonitor.swift:263` | Critical | Main thread stall, visible latency |
| 2 | Two synchronous shell processes (`netstat`) every 5s on main thread | `NetworkMonitor.swift:163` | Critical | 100-400ms main thread stall every 5s |
| 3 | Pretty-printed JSON encode of 20k records + write on every event | `HistoryStore.swift:117,137` | High | Unnecessary I/O + memory pressure |
| 4 | NSImage symbol recreation every second per menu bar item | `MenuBarController.swift:72,192` | High | Heap allocation churn at 1Hz |
| 5 | `fetchFrequency()` returns hardcoded `3.2` | `CPUMonitor.swift:167` | Medium | Misleading data stored to disk |
| 6 | PID buffer allocates 1024 elements and silently truncates at 256 PIDs | `ProcessMonitor.swift:42` | Medium | Missed processes + wasted allocation |
| 7 | Two full O(n log n) sorts every 3s | `ProcessMonitor.swift:86` | Medium | CPU cost on a loaded system |
| 8 | `getRouterIP()` called twice per 1s tick | `WiFiMonitor.swift:119,142` | Medium | 2x sysconfiguration store reads |
| 9 | `print` in 1s hot path with string interpolation | `WiFiMonitor.swift:88,170` | Low | GC pressure from transient strings |
| 10 | `hw.memsize` syscall every 2s | `MemoryMonitor.swift:56` | Low | Unnecessary kernel call, easily cached |
| 11 | Full filesystem enumeration at startup with no cooldown | `DiskMonitor.swift:39` | Medium | Startup I/O spike on large machines |
| 12 | `menuBarItems` string parsed on every 1s menu bar update | `AppSettings.swift:48` | Low | Unnecessary string allocation at 1Hz |
| 13 | `Array.removeFirst()` O(n) shift in all history arrays | `CPUMonitor.swift:43` (all monitors) | Low | Minor but systematic GC pressure |

### Highest-Priority Fixes (implement in order)

1. **`WiFiMonitor.measureDNSLookup()`** — move off main thread. Single biggest main-thread risk.
2. **`NetworkMonitor.fetchActiveConnectionsViaNetstat()`** — make async. Single biggest CPU/latency risk.
3. **`HistoryStore.persistToDisk()`** — remove `.prettyPrinted`, add 2s debounce on event-triggered writes.
4. **`MenuBarController`** — cache `NSImage` symbols and `menuBarItems` array.
5. **`MemoryMonitor`** — cache `hw.memsize` once.
6. **`WiFiMonitor`** — deduplicate `getRouterIP()` call and remove production `print` statements.
7. **`ProcessMonitor`** — fix PID buffer sizing and switch to partial sort.

**Fix:** Replace the history arrays with a `[T]` backed by a fixed-capacity circular write index, or use `history.dropFirst()` with `ArraySlice` and only reallocate periodically.

---

## Summary Table

| # | Issue | File | Severity | Impact |
|---|-------|------|----------|--------|
| 1 | Blocking DNS lookup on main thread every 1s | `WiFiMonitor.swift:263` | Critical | Main thread stall, visible latency |
| 2 | Two synchronous shell processes (`netstat`) every 5s on main thread | `NetworkMonitor.swift:163` | Critical | 100-400ms main thread stall every 5s |
| 3 | Pretty-printed JSON encode of 20k records + write on every event | `HistoryStore.swift:117,137` | High | Unnecessary I/O + memory pressure |
| 4 | NSImage symbol recreation every second per menu bar item | `MenuBarController.swift:72,192` | High | Heap allocation churn at 1Hz |
| 5 | `fetchFrequency()` returns hardcoded `3.2` | `CPUMonitor.swift:167` | Medium | Misleading data stored to disk |
| 6 | PID buffer allocates 1024 elements and silently truncates at 256 PIDs | `ProcessMonitor.swift:42` | Medium | Missed processes + wasted allocation |
| 7 | Two full O(n log n) sorts every 3s | `ProcessMonitor.swift:86` | Medium | CPU cost on a loaded system |
| 8 | `getRouterIP()` called twice per 1s tick | `WiFiMonitor.swift:119,142` | Medium | 2x sysconfiguration store reads |
| 9 | `print` in 1s hot path with string interpolation | `WiFiMonitor.swift:88,170` | Low | GC pressure from transient strings |
| 10 | `hw.memsize` syscall every 2s | `MemoryMonitor.swift:56` | Low | Unnecessary kernel call, easily cached |
| 11 | Full filesystem enumeration at startup with no cooldown | `DiskMonitor.swift:39` | Medium | Startup I/O spike on large machines |
| 12 | `menuBarItems` string parsed on every 1s menu bar update | `AppSettings.swift:48` | Low | Unnecessary string allocation at 1Hz |
| 13 | `Array.removeFirst()` O(n) shift in all history arrays | `CPUMonitor.swift:43` (all monitors) | Low | Minor but systematic GC pressure |

### Highest-Priority Fixes (implement in order)

1. **`WiFiMonitor.measureDNSLookup()`** — move off main thread. Single biggest main-thread risk.
2. **`NetworkMonitor.fetchActiveConnectionsViaNetstat()`** — make async. Single biggest CPU/latency risk.
3. **`HistoryStore.persistToDisk()`** — remove `.prettyPrinted`, add 2s debounce on event-triggered writes.
4. **`MenuBarController`** — cache `NSImage` symbols and `menuBarItems` array.
5. **`MemoryMonitor`** — cache `hw.memsize` once.
6. **`WiFiMonitor`** — deduplicate `getRouterIP()` call and remove production `print` statements.
7. **`ProcessMonitor`** — fix PID buffer sizing and switch to partial sort.

     5→class NetworkMonitor {
     6→    var current: NetworkMetrics?
     7→    var history: [NetworkMetrics] = []
     8→
     9→    private var timer: Timer?

    17→    func start(interval: TimeInterval = 1.0) {
    18→        timer?.invalidate()
    19→        previousBytesIn = 0
    20→        previousBytesOut = 0
    21→        previousSampleAt = nil
    22→        pathMonitor = NWPathMonitor()
    23→        pathMonitor?.start(queue: DispatchQueue.global(qos: .background))
    24→
    25→        timer = Timer.scheduledTimer(withTimeInterval: interval, repeats: true) { [weak self] _ in
    26→            self?.update()
    27→        }
    28→        update()
    29→    }

    36→    }

    38→    private func update() {

    40→
    41→        current = metrics

    43→
    44→        if history.count > 120 {
    45→            history.removeFirst()
    46→        }
    47→    }
    48→
    49→    private func fetchMetrics() -> NetworkMetrics? {
    50→        let now = Date()
    51→        var bytesIn: UInt64 = 0
    52→        var bytesOut: UInt64 = 0
    53→        var interfaceName = "en0"
    54→
    55→        if let addrs = getifaddrs_wrapper() {
    56→            var ptr: UnsafeMutablePointer<ifaddrs>? = addrs
    57→            while let current = ptr {
    58→                defer { ptr = current.pointee.ifa_next }
    59→
    60→                let interface = current.pointee
    61→                let name = String(cString: interface.ifa_name)
    62→
    63→                if name.starts(with: "en"), let data = interface.ifa_data {
    64→                    let networkData = data.assumingMemoryBound(to: if_data.self).pointee
    65→                    bytesIn = saturatingAdd(bytesIn, UInt64(networkData.ifi_ibytes))
    66→                    bytesOut = saturatingAdd(bytesOut, UInt64(networkData.ifi_obytes))
    67→                    interfaceName = name
    68→                }
    69→            }
    70→            freeifaddrs(addrs)
    71→        }
    72→
    73→        let elapsedSeconds: Double
    74→        if let previousSampleAt {
    75→            elapsedSeconds = max(now.timeIntervalSince(previousSampleAt), 0.001)
    76→        } else {
    77→            elapsedSeconds = 1.0
    78→        }
    79→
    80→        let uploadDelta = counterDelta(current: bytesOut, previous: previousBytesOut)
    81→        let downloadDelta = counterDelta(current: bytesIn, previous: previousBytesIn)
    82→        let uploadSpeed = previousSampleAt == nil ? 0 : Double(uploadDelta) / elapsedSeconds
    83→        let downloadSpeed = previousSampleAt == nil ? 0 : Double(downloadDelta) / elapsedSeconds
    84→
    85→        previousBytesIn = bytesIn
    86→        previousBytesOut = bytesOut
    87→        previousSampleAt = now
    88→
    89→        return NetworkMetrics(
    90→            timestamp: now,
    91→            uploadSpeed: uploadSpeed,
    92→            downloadSpeed: downloadSpeed,
    93→            interfaceName: interfaceName,
    94→            localIP: getLocalIP(),
    95→            publicIP: nil, // Fetched async
    96→            activeConnections: getActiveConnections()
    97→        )
    98→    }
    99→
   100→    private func counterDelta(current: UInt64, previous: UInt64) -> UInt64 {
   101→        guard previous > 0 else { return 0 }
   102→
   103→        if current >= previous {
   104→            return current - previous
   105→        }
   106→
   107→        // Interface counters can reset across reconnects or interface swaps.
   108→        // Treat as a restart to avoid overflow and unrealistic spikes.
   109→        return current
   110→    }
   111→
   112→    private func saturatingAdd(_ lhs: UInt64, _ rhs: UInt64) -> UInt64 {
   113→        let (value, overflowed) = lhs.addingReportingOverflow(rhs)
   114→        return overflowed ? UInt64.max : value
   115→    }
   116→
   117→    private func getifaddrs_wrapper() -> UnsafeMutablePointer<ifaddrs>? {
   118→        var addrs: UnsafeMutablePointer<ifaddrs>?
   119→        guard getifaddrs(&addrs) == 0 else { return nil }
   120→        return addrs
   121→    }
   122→
   123→    private func getLocalIP() -> String? {
   124→        var address: String?
   125→        var ifaddr: UnsafeMutablePointer<ifaddrs>?
   126→
   127→        if getifaddrs(&ifaddr) == 0 {
   128→            var ptr = ifaddr
   129→            while ptr != nil {
   130→                defer { ptr = ptr?.pointee.ifa_next }
   131→
   132→                let interface = ptr?.pointee
   133→                let addrFamily = interface?.ifa_addr.pointee.sa_family
   134→
   135→                if addrFamily == UInt8(AF_INET) {
   136→                    let name = String(cString: (interface?.ifa_name)!)
   137→                    if name == "en0" || name == "en1" {
   138→                        var hostname = [CChar](repeating: 0, count: Int(NI_MAXHOST))
   139→                        getnameinfo(interface?.ifa_addr, socklen_t((interface?.ifa_addr.pointee.sa_len)!),
   140→                                    &hostname, socklen_t(hostname.count),
   141→                                    nil, socklen_t(0), NI_NUMERICHOST)
   142→                        address = String(cString: hostname)
   143→                    }
   144→                }
   145→            }
   146→            freeifaddrs(ifaddr)
   147→        }
   148→        return address
   149→    }
   150→
   151→    private func getActiveConnections() -> Int {
   152→        let now = Date()
   153→        if now.timeIntervalSince(lastConnectionCountRefresh) < 5 {
   154→            return cachedActiveConnections
   155→        }
   156→
   157→        lastConnectionCountRefresh = now
   158→        cachedActiveConnections = fetchActiveConnectionsViaNetstat() ?? cachedActiveConnections
   159→        return cachedActiveConnections
   160→    }
   161→
   162→    private func fetchActiveConnectionsViaNetstat() -> Int? {
   163→        let tcpOutput = runCommand("/usr/sbin/netstat", arguments: ["-an", "-p", "tcp"]) ?? ""
   164→        let udpOutput = runCommand("/usr/sbin/netstat", arguments: ["-an", "-p", "udp"]) ?? ""
   165→
   166→        guard !tcpOutput.isEmpty || !udpOutput.isEmpty else { return nil }
   167→
   168→        let tcpCount = tcpOutput
   169→            .split(whereSeparator: \.isNewline)
   170→            .filter { line in
   171→                let text = line.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespaces)
   172→                guard text.hasPrefix("tcp") else { return false }
   173→
   174→                let cols = text.split(whereSeparator: \.isWhitespace)
   175→                guard let state = cols.last.map(String.init) else { return false }
   176→                return state != "LISTEN" && state != "CLOSED"
   177→            }
   178→            .count
   179→
   180→        let udpCount = udpOutput
   181→            .split(whereSeparator: \.isNewline)
   182→            .filter { line in
   183→                let text = line.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespaces)
   184→                return text.hasPrefix("udp")
   185→            }
   186→            .count
   187→
   188→        return tcpCount + udpCount
   189→    }
   190→
   191→    private func runCommand(_ executable: String, arguments: [String]) -> String? {
   192→        let process = Process()
   193→        let pipe = Pipe()
   194→        let errorPipe = Pipe()
   195→
   196→        process.executableURL = URL(fileURLWithPath: executable)
   197→        process.arguments = arguments
   198→        process.standardOutput = pipe
   199→        process.standardError = errorPipe
   200→
   201→        do {
   202→            try process.run()
   203→        } catch {
   204→            return nil
   205→        }
   206→
   207→        let data = pipe.fileHandleForReading.readDataToEndOfFile()
   208→        process.waitUntilExit()
   209→
   210→        guard process.terminationStatus == 0 else { return nil }
   211→        return String(data: data, encoding: .utf8)
   212→    }
   213→}
